records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for epidemic, severity: high, location: Philippines, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

A cholera outbreak has been declared in Philippines, with 8,480 confirmed cases and 1859 deaths reported across 18 provinces as of today. Case fatality rate stands at 2.5%. The outbreak originated in Manila and has spread to neighbouring areas.

HEALTH RESPONSE

- 36 treatment centers established
- 4,858 health workers deployed
- Contact tracing: 43,920 contacts identified and monitored
- Vaccination campaign targeting 769,843 people

CHALLENGES

Limited laboratory capacity, inadequate medical supplies, community resistance to health measures, and cross-border movement complicating containment efforts.

RESOURCE NEEDS

WHO has classified this as a Grade 3 emergency. Urgent need for medical supplies, PPE, diagnostic kits, and additional health personnel. Estimated funding gap: $143M.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for earthquake, severity: critical, location: Indonesia, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

A magnitude 6.7 earthquake struck Jakarta, Indonesia at 20:35 local time, with the epicentre located 54km underground. The quake caused widespread structural damage across the metropolitan area. 1299 deaths confirmed, 27,567 injured, and an estimated 421,657 people displaced from their homes.

DAMAGE ASSESSMENT

- 8,134 buildings collapsed or severely damaged
- Critical infrastructure: water main ruptured
- Hospitals operating at reduced capacity
- Power outages affecting 14,030 households
- Road access disrupted in 24 areas

HUMANITARIAN NEEDS

Immediate priorities include urban search and rescue, emergency medical care, temporary shelter, and restoration of water supply. Aftershocks continuing, complicating rescue efforts.

COORDINATION

UN OCHA has activated the cluster system. UNDAC team deployed. International search and rescue teams from 3 countries arriving. Government has requested international assistance.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for drought, severity: critical, location: Syria, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Prolonged drought conditions in Syria have entered their 14th consecutive month, with rainfall at 35% of seasonal average. An estimated 2,061,874 people are facing acute food insecurity across 10 regions.

FOOD SECURITY

- IPC Phase 3 (Crisis): 305,826 people
- IPC Phase 4 (Emergency): 335,880 people
- Livestock losses: 263,364 animals
- Crop production: 52% below average

WATER SITUATION

49% of water sources dried up or critically low. Water trucking operations serving 172,630 people daily but demand far exceeds current capacity.

HUMANITARIAN RESPONSE

FAO and WFP scaling up emergency food assistance. Supplementary feeding programmes targeting 138,953 malnourished children under 5. Cash transfer programmes reaching 32,383 households.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for flood, severity: critical, location: Ethiopia, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Heavy monsoon rains have caused severe flooding in Addis Ababa, Ethiopia, displacing approximately 385,458 people across 4 districts. Water levels have risen to 5.8m above normal, submerging residential areas and critical infrastructure. 1728 fatalities have been confirmed so far.

HUMANITARIAN NEEDS

- Emergency shelter for 385,458 displaced persons
- Clean drinking water — municipal supply contaminated
- Medical supplies: waterborne disease prevention kits
- Food supplies for an estimated 94,811 affected individuals
- Search and rescue operations in 27 submerged neighborhoods

RESPONSE ACTIONS

National disaster management authority has declared a state of emergency. Military helicopters deployed for evacuation. International aid agencies mobilising emergency response teams. Temporary shelters established at 22 locations including schools and community centers.

RESOURCE REQUIREMENTS

- Water purification units: 22
- Emergency tents: 17,535
- Medical teams: 22
- Rescue boats: 178
- Food ration packs: 51,631

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for epidemic, severity: high, location: South Sudan, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

A typhoid outbreak has been declared in South Sudan, with 44,833 confirmed cases and 1109 deaths reported across 18 provinces as of today. Case fatality rate stands at 14.9%. The outbreak originated in Juba and has spread to neighbouring areas.

HEALTH RESPONSE

- 23 treatment centers established
- 2,350 health workers deployed
- Contact tracing: 23,416 contacts identified and monitored
- Vaccination campaign targeting 504,750 people

CHALLENGES

Limited laboratory capacity, inadequate medical supplies, community resistance to health measures, and cross-border movement complicating containment efforts.

RESOURCE NEEDS

WHO has classified this as a Grade 3 emergency. Urgent need for medical supplies, PPE, diagnostic kits, and additional health personnel. Estimated funding gap: $192M.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for drought, severity: critical, location: Indonesia, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Prolonged drought conditions in Indonesia have entered their 14th consecutive month, with rainfall at 24% of seasonal average. An estimated 1,985,709 people are facing acute food insecurity across 12 regions.

FOOD SECURITY

- IPC Phase 3 (Crisis): 107,609 people
- IPC Phase 4 (Emergency): 311,493 people
- Livestock losses: 106,955 animals
- Crop production: 46% below average

WATER SITUATION

74% of water sources dried up or critically low. Water trucking operations serving 93,444 people daily but demand far exceeds current capacity.

HUMANITARIAN RESPONSE

FAO and WFP scaling up emergency food assistance. Supplementary feeding programmes targeting 57,953 malnourished children under 5. Cash transfer programmes reaching 96,463 households.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for flood, severity: critical, location: Japan, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Heavy monsoon rains have caused severe flooding in Tokyo, Japan, displacing approximately 472,542 people across 12 districts. Water levels have risen to 6.0m above normal, submerging residential areas and critical infrastructure. 457 fatalities have been confirmed so far.

HUMANITARIAN NEEDS

- Emergency shelter for 472,542 displaced persons
- Clean drinking water — municipal supply contaminated
- Medical supplies: waterborne disease prevention kits
- Food supplies for an estimated 141,730 affected individuals
- Search and rescue operations in 25 submerged neighborhoods

RESPONSE ACTIONS

National disaster management authority has declared a state of emergency. Military helicopters deployed for evacuation. International aid agencies mobilising emergency response teams. Temporary shelters established at 20 locations including schools and community centers.

RESOURCE REQUIREMENTS

- Water purification units: 44
- Emergency tents: 10,529
- Medical teams: 40
- Rescue boats: 112
- Food ration packs: 144,225

SITUATION OVERVIEW

A malaria outbreak has been declared in Philippines, with 36,815 confirmed cases and 879 deaths reported across 14 provinces as of today. Case fatality rate stands at 13.5%. The outbreak originated in Manila and has spread to neighbouring areas.

HEALTH RESPONSE

- 47 treatment centers established
- 3,777 health workers deployed
- Contact tracing: 3,349 contacts identified and monitored
- Vaccination campaign targeting 628,160 people

CHALLENGES

Limited laboratory capacity, inadequate medical supplies, community resistance to health measures, and cross-border movement complicating containment efforts.

RESOURCE NEEDS

WHO has classified this as a Grade 2 emergency. Urgent need for medical supplies, PPE, diagnostic kits, and additional health personnel. Estimated funding gap: $12M.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for cyclone, severity: high, location: Indonesia, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Tropical Cyclone Haiyan made landfall near Jakarta, Indonesia as a Category 2 storm with sustained winds of 165km/h. Storm surge of 2.0m reported along the coastline. 279 fatalities confirmed, with 169 persons still missing.

IMPACT

- 256,265 people evacuated from coastal communities
- Extensive damage to crops affecting 23,806 hectares
- Fishing fleet: 329 vessels damaged or destroyed
- Communication networks disrupted across 11 provinces

RESPONSE PRIORITIES

1. Search and rescue in affected coastal communities
2. Emergency shelter and food distribution
3. Restoration of clean water supply
4. Medical response — injury treatment and disease prevention
5. Debris clearance for road access

RESOURCE MOBILIZATION

Red Cross/Red Crescent has deployed 18 emergency response units. WFP pre-positioned stocks being distributed. Government allocated emergency fund of $431M for immediate relief operations.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for earthquake, severity: critical, location: South Sudan, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

A magnitude 8.1 earthquake struck Juba, South Sudan at 00:01 local time, with the epicentre located 20km underground. The quake caused widespread structural damage across the metropolitan area. 1708 deaths confirmed, 36,406 injured, and an estimated 128,224 people displaced from their homes.

DAMAGE ASSESSMENT

- 4,145 buildings collapsed or severely damaged
- Critical infrastructure: bridges damaged
- Hospitals operating at reduced capacity
- Power outages affecting 36,347 households
- Road access disrupted in 32 areas

HUMANITARIAN NEEDS

Immediate priorities include urban search and rescue, emergency medical care, temporary shelter, and restoration of water supply. Aftershocks continuing, complicating rescue efforts.

COORDINATION

UN OCHA has activated the cluster system. UNDAC team deployed. International search and rescue teams from 14 countries arriving. Government has requested international assistance.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for flood, severity: critical, location: Indonesia, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Heavy monsoon rains have caused severe flooding in Jakarta, Indonesia, displacing approximately 444,927 people across 24 districts. Water levels have risen to 7.1m above normal, submerging residential areas and critical infrastructure. 881 fatalities have been confirmed so far.

HUMANITARIAN NEEDS

- Emergency shelter for 444,927 displaced persons
- Clean drinking water — municipal supply contaminated
- Medical supplies: waterborne disease prevention kits
- Food supplies for an estimated 96,706 affected individuals
- Search and rescue operations in 29 submerged neighborhoods

RESPONSE ACTIONS

National disaster management authority has declared a state of emergency. Military helicopters deployed for evacuation. International aid agencies mobilising emergency response teams. Temporary shelters established at 52 locations including schools and community centers.

RESOURCE REQUIREMENTS

- Water purification units: 116
- Emergency tents: 4,812
- Medical teams: 20
- Rescue boats: 148
- Food ration packs: 162,497

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for drought, severity: critical, location: South Sudan, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Prolonged drought conditions in South Sudan have entered their 14th consecutive month, with rainfall at 30% of seasonal average. An estimated 3,289,538 people are facing acute food insecurity across 3 regions.

FOOD SECURITY

- IPC Phase 3 (Crisis): 1,832,080 people
- IPC Phase 4 (Emergency): 262,930 people
- Livestock losses: 269,994 animals
- Crop production: 44% below average

WATER SITUATION

67% of water sources dried up or critically low. Water trucking operations serving 142,689 people daily but demand far exceeds current capacity.

HUMANITARIAN RESPONSE

FAO and WFP scaling up emergency food assistance. Supplementary feeding programmes targeting 61,485 malnourished children under 5. Cash transfer programmes reaching 28,671 households.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for cyclone, severity: high, location: Turkey, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Tropical Cyclone Dorian made landfall near Istanbul, Turkey as a Category 2 storm with sustained winds of 241km/h. Storm surge of 6.6m reported along the coastline. 302 fatalities confirmed, with 403 persons still missing.

IMPACT

- 363,301 people evacuated from coastal communities
- Extensive damage to crops affecting 103,484 hectares
- Fishing fleet: 1628 vessels damaged or destroyed
- Communication networks disrupted across 10 provinces

RESPONSE PRIORITIES

1. Search and rescue in affected coastal communities
2. Emergency shelter and food distribution
3. Restoration of clean water supply
4. Medical response — injury treatment and disease prevention
5. Debris clearance for road access

RESOURCE MOBILIZATION

Red Cross/Red Crescent has deployed 16 emergency response units. WFP pre-positioned stocks being distributed. Government allocated emergency fund of $63M for immediate relief operations.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for earthquake, severity: critical, location: Syria, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

A magnitude 5.8 earthquake struck Aleppo, Syria at 01:48 local time, with the epicentre located 21km underground. The quake caused widespread structural damage across the metropolitan area. 618 deaths confirmed, 43,203 injured, and an estimated 338,209 people displaced from their homes.

DAMAGE ASSESSMENT

- 3,368 buildings collapsed or severely damaged
- Critical infrastructure: major hospital partially collapsed
- Hospitals operating at reduced capacity
- Power outages affecting 50,124 households
- Road access disrupted in 40 areas

HUMANITARIAN NEEDS

Immediate priorities include urban search and rescue, emergency medical care, temporary shelter, and restoration of water supply. Aftershocks continuing, complicating rescue efforts.

COORDINATION

UN OCHA has activated the cluster system. UNDAC team deployed. International search and rescue teams from 8 countries arriving. Government has requested international assistance.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for flood, severity: critical, location: Yemen, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Heavy monsoon rains have caused severe flooding in Sanaa, Yemen, displacing approximately 296,975 people across 25 districts. Water levels have risen to 3.9m above normal, submerging residential areas and critical infrastructure. 1252 fatalities have been confirmed so far.

HUMANITARIAN NEEDS

- Emergency shelter for 296,975 displaced persons
- Clean drinking water — municipal supply contaminated
- Medical supplies: waterborne disease prevention kits
- Food supplies for an estimated 189,771 affected individuals
- Search and rescue operations in 28 submerged neighborhoods

RESPONSE ACTIONS

National disaster management authority has declared a state of emergency. Military helicopters deployed for evacuation. International aid agencies mobilising emergency response teams. Temporary shelters established at 74 locations including schools and community centers.

RESOURCE REQUIREMENTS

- Water purification units: 119
- Emergency tents: 7,489
- Medical teams: 22
- Rescue boats: 103
- Food ration packs: 79,995

SITUATION OVERVIEW

A magnitude 7.8 earthquake struck Port-au-Prince, Haiti at 12:12 local time, with the epicentre located 5km underground. The quake caused widespread structural damage across the metropolitan area. 1505 deaths confirmed, 11,344 injured, and an estimated 155,227 people displaced from their homes.

DAMAGE ASSESSMENT

- 28,217 buildings collapsed or severely damaged
- Critical infrastructure: bridges damaged
- Hospitals operating at reduced capacity
- Power outages affecting 22,992 households
- Road access disrupted in 37 areas

HUMANITARIAN NEEDS

Immediate priorities include urban search and rescue, emergency medical care, temporary shelter, and restoration of water supply. Aftershocks continuing, complicating rescue efforts.

COORDINATION

UN OCHA has activated the cluster system. UNDAC team deployed. International search and rescue teams from 8 countries arriving. Government has requested international assistance.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for drought, severity: critical, location: Somalia, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Prolonged drought conditions in Somalia have entered their 15th consecutive month, with rainfall at 37% of seasonal average. An estimated 4,962,142 people are facing acute food insecurity across 11 regions.

FOOD SECURITY

- IPC Phase 3 (Crisis): 1,749,859 people
- IPC Phase 4 (Emergency): 258,963 people
- Livestock losses: 114,481 animals
- Crop production: 75% below average

WATER SITUATION

81% of water sources dried up or critically low. Water trucking operations serving 141,723 people daily but demand far exceeds current capacity.

HUMANITARIAN RESPONSE

FAO and WFP scaling up emergency food assistance. Supplementary feeding programmes targeting 75,189 malnourished children under 5. Cash transfer programmes reaching 13,025 households.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for cyclone, severity: high, location: Philippines, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Tropical Cyclone Amphan made landfall near Manila, Philippines as a Category 4 storm with sustained winds of 251km/h. Storm surge of 6.9m reported along the coastline. 446 fatalities confirmed, with 207 persons still missing.

IMPACT

- 110,385 people evacuated from coastal communities
- Extensive damage to crops affecting 132,020 hectares
- Fishing fleet: 108 vessels damaged or destroyed
- Communication networks disrupted across 15 provinces

RESPONSE PRIORITIES

1. Search and rescue in affected coastal communities
2. Emergency shelter and food distribution
3. Restoration of clean water supply
4. Medical response — injury treatment and disease prevention
5. Debris clearance for road access

RESOURCE MOBILIZATION

Red Cross/Red Crescent has deployed 18 emergency response units. WFP pre-positioned stocks being distributed. Government allocated emergency fund of $82M for immediate relief operations.

A magnitude 7.5 earthquake struck Karachi, Pakistan at 00:54 local time, with the epicentre located 69km underground. The quake caused widespread structural damage across the metropolitan area. 1691 deaths confirmed, 17,163 injured, and an estimated 128,753 people displaced from their homes.

- Critical infrastructure: airport runway cracked
- Hospitals operating at reduced capacity
- Power outages affecting 67,815 households
- Road access disrupted in 13 areas

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for flood, severity: critical, location: Bangladesh, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Heavy monsoon rains have caused severe flooding in Dhaka, Bangladesh, displacing approximately 403,282 people across 21 districts. Water levels have risen to 7.4m above normal, submerging residential areas and critical infrastructure. 870 fatalities have been confirmed so far.

HUMANITARIAN NEEDS

- Emergency shelter for 403,282 displaced persons
- Clean drinking water — municipal supply contaminated
- Medical supplies: waterborne disease prevention kits
- Food supplies for an estimated 48,713 affected individuals
- Search and rescue operations in 26 submerged neighborhoods

RESPONSE ACTIONS

National disaster management authority has declared a state of emergency. Military helicopters deployed for evacuation. International aid agencies mobilising emergency response teams. Temporary shelters established at 66 locations including schools and community centers.

RESOURCE REQUIREMENTS

- Water purification units: 125
- Emergency tents: 8,424
- Medical teams: 48
- Rescue boats: 165
- Food ration packs: 320,327

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for epidemic, severity: high, location: India, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

A Ebola outbreak has been declared in India, with 13,068 confirmed cases and 1353 deaths reported across 8 provinces as of today. Case fatality rate stands at 4.4%. The outbreak originated in Mumbai and has spread to neighbouring areas.

HEALTH RESPONSE

- 20 treatment centers established
- 4,884 health workers deployed
- Contact tracing: 32,676 contacts identified and monitored
- Vaccination campaign targeting 223,576 people

CHALLENGES

Limited laboratory capacity, inadequate medical supplies, community resistance to health measures, and cross-border movement complicating containment efforts.

RESOURCE NEEDS

WHO has classified this as a Grade 2 emergency. Urgent need for medical supplies, PPE, diagnostic kits, and additional health personnel. Estimated funding gap: $147M.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for earthquake, severity: critical, location: Yemen, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

A magnitude 7.4 earthquake struck Sanaa, Yemen at 09:49 local time, with the epicentre located 43km underground. The quake caused widespread structural damage across the metropolitan area. 1417 deaths confirmed, 11,333 injured, and an estimated 220,285 people displaced from their homes.

DAMAGE ASSESSMENT

- 8,097 buildings collapsed or severely damaged
- Critical infrastructure: bridges damaged
- Hospitals operating at reduced capacity
- Power outages affecting 318,344 households
- Road access disrupted in 31 areas

HUMANITARIAN NEEDS

Immediate priorities include urban search and rescue, emergency medical care, temporary shelter, and restoration of water supply. Aftershocks continuing, complicating rescue efforts.

COORDINATION

UN OCHA has activated the cluster system. UNDAC team deployed. International search and rescue teams from 4 countries arriving. Government has requested international assistance.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for cyclone, severity: high, location: India, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Tropical Cyclone Irma made landfall near Mumbai, India as a Category 4 storm with sustained winds of 153km/h. Storm surge of 5.5m reported along the coastline. 1013 fatalities confirmed, with 65 persons still missing.

IMPACT

- 320,331 people evacuated from coastal communities
- Extensive damage to crops affecting 88,947 hectares
- Fishing fleet: 687 vessels damaged or destroyed
- Communication networks disrupted across 3 provinces

RESPONSE PRIORITIES

1. Search and rescue in affected coastal communities
2. Emergency shelter and food distribution
3. Restoration of clean water supply
4. Medical response — injury treatment and disease prevention
5. Debris clearance for road access

RESOURCE MOBILIZATION

Red Cross/Red Crescent has deployed 25 emergency response units. WFP pre-positioned stocks being distributed. Government allocated emergency fund of $159M for immediate relief operations.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for drought, severity: critical, location: Nigeria, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Prolonged drought conditions in Nigeria have entered their 12th consecutive month, with rainfall at 25% of seasonal average. An estimated 4,191,577 people are facing acute food insecurity across 12 regions.

FOOD SECURITY

- IPC Phase 3 (Crisis): 1,163,000 people
- IPC Phase 4 (Emergency): 389,429 people
- Livestock losses: 482,328 animals
- Crop production: 39% below average

WATER SITUATION

62% of water sources dried up or critically low. Water trucking operations serving 96,269 people daily but demand far exceeds current capacity.

HUMANITARIAN RESPONSE

FAO and WFP scaling up emergency food assistance. Supplementary feeding programmes targeting 154,759 malnourished children under 5. Cash transfer programmes reaching 64,695 households.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for epidemic, severity: high, location: Somalia, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

A cholera outbreak has been declared in Somalia, with 48,054 confirmed cases and 257 deaths reported across 12 provinces as of today. Case fatality rate stands at 3.6%. The outbreak originated in Mogadishu and has spread to neighbouring areas.

HEALTH RESPONSE

- 27 treatment centers established
- 2,091 health workers deployed
- Contact tracing: 38,760 contacts identified and monitored
- Vaccination campaign targeting 1,930,331 people

CHALLENGES

Limited laboratory capacity, inadequate medical supplies, community resistance to health measures, and cross-border movement complicating containment efforts.

RESOURCE NEEDS

WHO has classified this as a Grade 3 emergency. Urgent need for medical supplies, PPE, diagnostic kits, and additional health personnel. Estimated funding gap: $163M.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for flood, severity: critical, location: Turkey, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Heavy monsoon rains have caused severe flooding in Istanbul, Turkey, displacing approximately 363,234 people across 18 districts. Water levels have risen to 7.6m above normal, submerging residential areas and critical infrastructure. 1882 fatalities have been confirmed so far.

HUMANITARIAN NEEDS

- Emergency shelter for 363,234 displaced persons
- Clean drinking water — municipal supply contaminated
- Medical supplies: waterborne disease prevention kits
- Food supplies for an estimated 55,977 affected individuals
- Search and rescue operations in 28 submerged neighborhoods

RESPONSE ACTIONS

National disaster management authority has declared a state of emergency. Military helicopters deployed for evacuation. International aid agencies mobilising emergency response teams. Temporary shelters established at 99 locations including schools and community centers.

RESOURCE REQUIREMENTS

- Water purification units: 176
- Emergency tents: 16,270
- Medical teams: 27
- Rescue boats: 198
- Food ration packs: 434,981

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for epidemic, severity: high, location: Kenya, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

A malaria outbreak has been declared in Kenya, with 5,854 confirmed cases and 1085 deaths reported across 3 provinces as of today. Case fatality rate stands at 0.6%. The outbreak originated in Nairobi and has spread to neighbouring areas.

HEALTH RESPONSE

- 27 treatment centers established
- 788 health workers deployed
- Contact tracing: 14,727 contacts identified and monitored
- Vaccination campaign targeting 380,310 people

CHALLENGES

Limited laboratory capacity, inadequate medical supplies, community resistance to health measures, and cross-border movement complicating containment efforts.

RESOURCE NEEDS

WHO has classified this as a Grade 2 emergency. Urgent need for medical supplies, PPE, diagnostic kits, and additional health personnel. Estimated funding gap: $131M.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for drought, severity: critical, location: Kenya, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Prolonged drought conditions in Kenya have entered their 4th consecutive month, with rainfall at 43% of seasonal average. An estimated 3,384,428 people are facing acute food insecurity across 7 regions.

FOOD SECURITY

- IPC Phase 3 (Crisis): 1,397,324 people
- IPC Phase 4 (Emergency): 269,085 people
- Livestock losses: 493,186 animals
- Crop production: 52% below average

WATER SITUATION

42% of water sources dried up or critically low. Water trucking operations serving 132,331 people daily but demand far exceeds current capacity.

HUMANITARIAN RESPONSE

FAO and WFP scaling up emergency food assistance. Supplementary feeding programmes targeting 30,933 malnourished children under 5. Cash transfer programmes reaching 53,428 households.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for earthquake, severity: critical, location: Philippines, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

A magnitude 7.8 earthquake struck Manila, Philippines at 06:39 local time, with the epicentre located 24km underground. The quake caused widespread structural damage across the metropolitan area. 1440 deaths confirmed, 39,260 injured, and an estimated 154,498 people displaced from their homes.

DAMAGE ASSESSMENT

- 3,747 buildings collapsed or severely damaged
- Critical infrastructure: major hospital partially collapsed
- Hospitals operating at reduced capacity
- Power outages affecting 379,736 households
- Road access disrupted in 13 areas

HUMANITARIAN NEEDS

Immediate priorities include urban search and rescue, emergency medical care, temporary shelter, and restoration of water supply. Aftershocks continuing, complicating rescue efforts.

COORDINATION

UN OCHA has activated the cluster system. UNDAC team deployed. International search and rescue teams from 14 countries arriving. Government has requested international assistance.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for cyclone, severity: high, location: Afghanistan, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Tropical Cyclone Fani made landfall near Kabul, Afghanistan as a Category 2 storm with sustained winds of 296km/h. Storm surge of 2.7m reported along the coastline. 376 fatalities confirmed, with 330 persons still missing.

IMPACT

- 250,677 people evacuated from coastal communities
- Extensive damage to crops affecting 193,429 hectares
- Fishing fleet: 425 vessels damaged or destroyed
- Communication networks disrupted across 9 provinces

RESPONSE PRIORITIES

1. Search and rescue in affected coastal communities
2. Emergency shelter and food distribution
3. Restoration of clean water supply
4. Medical response — injury treatment and disease prevention
5. Debris clearance for road access

RESOURCE MOBILIZATION

Red Cross/Red Crescent has deployed 17 emergency response units. WFP pre-positioned stocks being distributed. Government allocated emergency fund of $269M for immediate relief operations.

Heavy monsoon rains have caused severe flooding in Sanaa, Yemen, displacing approximately 331,958 people across 19 districts. Water levels have risen to 7.2m above normal, submerging residential areas and critical infrastructure. 1167 fatalities have been confirmed so far.

HUMANITARIAN NEEDS

- Medical supplies: waterborne disease prevention kits

- Medical teams: 31
- Rescue boats: 85
- Food ration packs: 477,796

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for epidemic, severity: high, location: South Sudan, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

A malaria outbreak has been declared in South Sudan, with 28,753 confirmed cases and 1015 deaths reported across 10 provinces as of today. Case fatality rate stands at 16.0%. The outbreak originated in Juba and has spread to neighbouring areas.

HEALTH RESPONSE

- 9 treatment centers established
- 4,195 health workers deployed
- Contact tracing: 39,060 contacts identified and monitored
- Vaccination campaign targeting 1,033,246 people

CHALLENGES

Limited laboratory capacity, inadequate medical supplies, community resistance to health measures, and cross-border movement complicating containment efforts.

RESOURCE NEEDS

WHO has classified this as a Grade 3 emergency. Urgent need for medical supplies, PPE, diagnostic kits, and additional health personnel. Estimated funding gap: $86M.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for earthquake, severity: critical, location: Philippines, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

A magnitude 5.7 earthquake struck Manila, Philippines at 21:40 local time, with the epicentre located 27km underground. The quake caused widespread structural damage across the metropolitan area. 1195 deaths confirmed, 38,659 injured, and an estimated 270,050 people displaced from their homes.

DAMAGE ASSESSMENT

- 13,584 buildings collapsed or severely damaged
- Critical infrastructure: airport runway cracked
- Hospitals operating at reduced capacity
- Power outages affecting 217,178 households
- Road access disrupted in 20 areas

HUMANITARIAN NEEDS

Immediate priorities include urban search and rescue, emergency medical care, temporary shelter, and restoration of water supply. Aftershocks continuing, complicating rescue efforts.

COORDINATION

UN OCHA has activated the cluster system. UNDAC team deployed. International search and rescue teams from 5 countries arriving. Government has requested international assistance.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for cyclone, severity: high, location: Japan, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Tropical Cyclone Haiyan made landfall near Tokyo, Japan as a Category 5 storm with sustained winds of 132km/h. Storm surge of 2.4m reported along the coastline. 559 fatalities confirmed, with 272 persons still missing.

IMPACT

- 349,458 people evacuated from coastal communities
- Extensive damage to crops affecting 199,943 hectares
- Fishing fleet: 920 vessels damaged or destroyed
- Communication networks disrupted across 13 provinces

RESPONSE PRIORITIES

1. Search and rescue in affected coastal communities
2. Emergency shelter and food distribution
3. Restoration of clean water supply
4. Medical response — injury treatment and disease prevention
5. Debris clearance for road access

RESOURCE MOBILIZATION

Red Cross/Red Crescent has deployed 11 emergency response units. WFP pre-positioned stocks being distributed. Government allocated emergency fund of $375M for immediate relief operations.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for epidemic, severity: high, location: Haiti, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

A Ebola outbreak has been declared in Haiti, with 49,063 confirmed cases and 667 deaths reported across 19 provinces as of today. Case fatality rate stands at 3.4%. The outbreak originated in Port-au-Prince and has spread to neighbouring areas.

HEALTH RESPONSE

- 24 treatment centers established
- 3,050 health workers deployed
- Contact tracing: 23,997 contacts identified and monitored
- Vaccination campaign targeting 1,491,204 people

CHALLENGES

Limited laboratory capacity, inadequate medical supplies, community resistance to health measures, and cross-border movement complicating containment efforts.

RESOURCE NEEDS

WHO has classified this as a Grade 3 emergency. Urgent need for medical supplies, PPE, diagnostic kits, and additional health personnel. Estimated funding gap: $167M.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for drought, severity: critical, location: Somalia, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Prolonged drought conditions in Somalia have entered their 9th consecutive month, with rainfall at 16% of seasonal average. An estimated 2,997,892 people are facing acute food insecurity across 5 regions.

FOOD SECURITY

- IPC Phase 3 (Crisis): 783,101 people
- IPC Phase 4 (Emergency): 231,586 people
- Livestock losses: 87,402 animals
- Crop production: 43% below average

WATER SITUATION

42% of water sources dried up or critically low. Water trucking operations serving 119,276 people daily but demand far exceeds current capacity.

HUMANITARIAN RESPONSE

FAO and WFP scaling up emergency food assistance. Supplementary feeding programmes targeting 34,245 malnourished children under 5. Cash transfer programmes reaching 50,753 households.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for earthquake, severity: critical, location: Japan, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

A magnitude 7.8 earthquake struck Tokyo, Japan at 15:47 local time, with the epicentre located 20km underground. The quake caused widespread structural damage across the metropolitan area. 1323 deaths confirmed, 23,070 injured, and an estimated 282,662 people displaced from their homes.

DAMAGE ASSESSMENT

- 13,824 buildings collapsed or severely damaged
- Critical infrastructure: major hospital partially collapsed
- Hospitals operating at reduced capacity
- Power outages affecting 402,451 households
- Road access disrupted in 32 areas

HUMANITARIAN NEEDS

Immediate priorities include urban search and rescue, emergency medical care, temporary shelter, and restoration of water supply. Aftershocks continuing, complicating rescue efforts.

COORDINATION

UN OCHA has activated the cluster system. UNDAC team deployed. International search and rescue teams from 7 countries arriving. Government has requested international assistance.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for cyclone, severity: high, location: Pakistan, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Tropical Cyclone Irma made landfall near Karachi, Pakistan as a Category 3 storm with sustained winds of 299km/h. Storm surge of 3.2m reported along the coastline. 459 fatalities confirmed, with 199 persons still missing.

IMPACT

- 302,075 people evacuated from coastal communities
- Extensive damage to crops affecting 8,536 hectares
- Fishing fleet: 1061 vessels damaged or destroyed
- Communication networks disrupted across 13 provinces

RESPONSE PRIORITIES

1. Search and rescue in affected coastal communities
2. Emergency shelter and food distribution
3. Restoration of clean water supply
4. Medical response — injury treatment and disease prevention
5. Debris clearance for road access

RESOURCE MOBILIZATION

Red Cross/Red Crescent has deployed 16 emergency response units. WFP pre-positioned stocks being distributed. Government allocated emergency fund of $80M for immediate relief operations.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for flood, severity: critical, location: Yemen, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Heavy monsoon rains have caused severe flooding in Sanaa, Yemen, displacing approximately 84,408 people across 19 districts. Water levels have risen to 4.5m above normal, submerging residential areas and critical infrastructure. 508 fatalities have been confirmed so far.

HUMANITARIAN NEEDS

- Emergency shelter for 84,408 displaced persons
- Clean drinking water — municipal supply contaminated
- Medical supplies: waterborne disease prevention kits
- Food supplies for an estimated 128,598 affected individuals
- Search and rescue operations in 19 submerged neighborhoods

RESPONSE ACTIONS

National disaster management authority has declared a state of emergency. Military helicopters deployed for evacuation. International aid agencies mobilising emergency response teams. Temporary shelters established at 70 locations including schools and community centers.

RESOURCE REQUIREMENTS

- Water purification units: 122
- Emergency tents: 14,461
- Medical teams: 31
- Rescue boats: 143
- Food ration packs: 250,885

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for epidemic, severity: high, location: Japan, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

A dengue fever outbreak has been declared in Japan, with 16,699 confirmed cases and 1187 deaths reported across 18 provinces as of today. Case fatality rate stands at 20.5%. The outbreak originated in Tokyo and has spread to neighbouring areas.

HEALTH RESPONSE

- 15 treatment centers established
- 4,526 health workers deployed
- Contact tracing: 15,778 contacts identified and monitored
- Vaccination campaign targeting 1,736,264 people

CHALLENGES

Limited laboratory capacity, inadequate medical supplies, community resistance to health measures, and cross-border movement complicating containment efforts.

RESOURCE NEEDS

WHO has classified this as a Grade 1 emergency. Urgent need for medical supplies, PPE, diagnostic kits, and additional health personnel. Estimated funding gap: $28M.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for cyclone, severity: high, location: Somalia, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Tropical Cyclone Pam made landfall near Mogadishu, Somalia as a Category 2 storm with sustained winds of 180km/h. Storm surge of 6.2m reported along the coastline. 1919 fatalities confirmed, with 239 persons still missing.

IMPACT

- 437,333 people evacuated from coastal communities
- Extensive damage to crops affecting 197,437 hectares
- Fishing fleet: 747 vessels damaged or destroyed
- Communication networks disrupted across 9 provinces

RESPONSE PRIORITIES

1. Search and rescue in affected coastal communities
2. Emergency shelter and food distribution
3. Restoration of clean water supply
4. Medical response — injury treatment and disease prevention
5. Debris clearance for road access

RESOURCE MOBILIZATION

Red Cross/Red Crescent has deployed 9 emergency response units. WFP pre-positioned stocks being distributed. Government allocated emergency fund of $412M for immediate relief operations.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for epidemic, severity: high, location: Afghanistan, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

A typhoid outbreak has been declared in Afghanistan, with 12,351 confirmed cases and 1948 deaths reported across 16 provinces as of today. Case fatality rate stands at 17.0%. The outbreak originated in Kabul and has spread to neighbouring areas.

HEALTH RESPONSE

- 38 treatment centers established
- 3,810 health workers deployed
- Contact tracing: 25,545 contacts identified and monitored
- Vaccination campaign targeting 1,505,051 people

CHALLENGES

Limited laboratory capacity, inadequate medical supplies, community resistance to health measures, and cross-border movement complicating containment efforts.

RESOURCE NEEDS

WHO has classified this as a Grade 3 emergency. Urgent need for medical supplies, PPE, diagnostic kits, and additional health personnel. Estimated funding gap: $88M.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for earthquake, severity: critical, location: Nigeria, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

A magnitude 7.6 earthquake struck Lagos, Nigeria at 19:17 local time, with the epicentre located 5km underground. The quake caused widespread structural damage across the metropolitan area. 382 deaths confirmed, 9,181 injured, and an estimated 411,700 people displaced from their homes.

DAMAGE ASSESSMENT

- 10,777 buildings collapsed or severely damaged
- Critical infrastructure: major hospital partially collapsed
- Hospitals operating at reduced capacity
- Power outages affecting 393,543 households
- Road access disrupted in 29 areas

HUMANITARIAN NEEDS

Immediate priorities include urban search and rescue, emergency medical care, temporary shelter, and restoration of water supply. Aftershocks continuing, complicating rescue efforts.

COORDINATION

UN OCHA has activated the cluster system. UNDAC team deployed. International search and rescue teams from 14 countries arriving. Government has requested international assistance.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for cyclone, severity: high, location: Ethiopia, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Tropical Cyclone Haiyan made landfall near Addis Ababa, Ethiopia as a Category 4 storm with sustained winds of 260km/h. Storm surge of 3.1m reported along the coastline. 1475 fatalities confirmed, with 248 persons still missing.

IMPACT

- 278,066 people evacuated from coastal communities
- Extensive damage to crops affecting 38,126 hectares
- Fishing fleet: 1147 vessels damaged or destroyed
- Communication networks disrupted across 13 provinces

RESPONSE PRIORITIES

1. Search and rescue in affected coastal communities
2. Emergency shelter and food distribution
3. Restoration of clean water supply
4. Medical response — injury treatment and disease prevention
5. Debris clearance for road access

RESOURCE MOBILIZATION

Red Cross/Red Crescent has deployed 30 emergency response units. WFP pre-positioned stocks being distributed. Government allocated emergency fund of $46M for immediate relief operations.

A magnitude 7.2 earthquake struck Port-au-Prince, Haiti at 00:53 local time, with the epicentre located 18km underground. The quake caused widespread structural damage across the metropolitan area. 424 deaths confirmed, 45,525 injured, and an estimated 441,303 people displaced from their homes.

DAMAGE ASSESSMENT

- 13,848 buildings collapsed or severely damaged
- Critical infrastructure: water main ruptured
- Hospitals operating at reduced capacity
- Power outages affecting 116,206 households
- Road access disrupted in 31 areas

HUMANITARIAN NEEDS

Immediate priorities include urban search and rescue, emergency medical care, temporary shelter, and restoration of water supply. Aftershocks continuing, complicating rescue efforts.

COORDINATION

UN OCHA has activated the cluster system. UNDAC team deployed. International search and rescue teams from 8 countries arriving. Government has requested international assistance.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for cyclone, severity: high, location: Philippines, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Tropical Cyclone Fani made landfall near Manila, Philippines as a Category 4 storm with sustained winds of 262km/h. Storm surge of 7.0m reported along the coastline. 156 fatalities confirmed, with 404 persons still missing.

IMPACT

- 216,572 people evacuated from coastal communities
- Extensive damage to crops affecting 197,530 hectares
- Fishing fleet: 1728 vessels damaged or destroyed
- Communication networks disrupted across 4 provinces

RESPONSE PRIORITIES

1. Search and rescue in affected coastal communities
2. Emergency shelter and food distribution
3. Restoration of clean water supply
4. Medical response — injury treatment and disease prevention
5. Debris clearance for road access

RESOURCE MOBILIZATION

Red Cross/Red Crescent has deployed 13 emergency response units. WFP pre-positioned stocks being distributed. Government allocated emergency fund of $224M for immediate relief operations.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for epidemic, severity: high, location: Haiti, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

A dengue fever outbreak has been declared in Haiti, with 32,485 confirmed cases and 791 deaths reported across 11 provinces as of today. Case fatality rate stands at 13.7%. The outbreak originated in Port-au-Prince and has spread to neighbouring areas.

HEALTH RESPONSE

- 34 treatment centers established
- 4,948 health workers deployed
- Contact tracing: 29,682 contacts identified and monitored
- Vaccination campaign targeting 954,038 people

CHALLENGES

Limited laboratory capacity, inadequate medical supplies, community resistance to health measures, and cross-border movement complicating containment efforts.

RESOURCE NEEDS

WHO has classified this as a Grade 1 emergency. Urgent need for medical supplies, PPE, diagnostic kits, and additional health personnel. Estimated funding gap: $69M.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for drought, severity: critical, location: Somalia, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Prolonged drought conditions in Somalia have entered their 17th consecutive month, with rainfall at 42% of seasonal average. An estimated 2,509,740 people are facing acute food insecurity across 7 regions.

FOOD SECURITY

- IPC Phase 3 (Crisis): 418,072 people
- IPC Phase 4 (Emergency): 107,597 people
- Livestock losses: 39,523 animals
- Crop production: 31% below average

WATER SITUATION

85% of water sources dried up or critically low. Water trucking operations serving 87,963 people daily but demand far exceeds current capacity.

HUMANITARIAN RESPONSE

FAO and WFP scaling up emergency food assistance. Supplementary feeding programmes targeting 29,704 malnourished children under 5. Cash transfer programmes reaching 96,893 households.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for flood, severity: critical, location: Pakistan, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Heavy monsoon rains have caused severe flooding in Karachi, Pakistan, displacing approximately 234,239 people across 25 districts. Water levels have risen to 6.1m above normal, submerging residential areas and critical infrastructure. 1177 fatalities have been confirmed so far.

HUMANITARIAN NEEDS

- Emergency shelter for 234,239 displaced persons
- Clean drinking water — municipal supply contaminated
- Medical supplies: waterborne disease prevention kits
- Food supplies for an estimated 6,537 affected individuals
- Search and rescue operations in 16 submerged neighborhoods

RESPONSE ACTIONS

National disaster management authority has declared a state of emergency. Military helicopters deployed for evacuation. International aid agencies mobilising emergency response teams. Temporary shelters established at 68 locations including schools and community centers.

RESOURCE REQUIREMENTS

- Water purification units: 22
- Emergency tents: 9,120
- Medical teams: 24
- Rescue boats: 153
- Food ration packs: 472,198

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for drought, severity: critical, location: Colombia, date: 2026-03-03

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for cyclone, severity: high, location: Bangladesh, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Tropical Cyclone Nargis made landfall near Dhaka, Bangladesh as a Category 2 storm with sustained winds of 140km/h. Storm surge of 7.5m reported along the coastline. 1898 fatalities confirmed, with 53 persons still missing.

IMPACT

- 165,849 people evacuated from coastal communities
- Extensive damage to crops affecting 113,278 hectares
- Fishing fleet: 744 vessels damaged or destroyed
- Communication networks disrupted across 15 provinces

RESPONSE PRIORITIES

1. Search and rescue in affected coastal communities
2. Emergency shelter and food distribution
3. Restoration of clean water supply
4. Medical response — injury treatment and disease prevention
5. Debris clearance for road access

RESOURCE MOBILIZATION

Red Cross/Red Crescent has deployed 15 emergency response units. WFP pre-positioned stocks being distributed. Government allocated emergency fund of $62M for immediate relief operations.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for flood, severity: critical, location: Mozambique, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Heavy monsoon rains have caused severe flooding in Maputo, Mozambique, displacing approximately 18,334 people across 3 districts. Water levels have risen to 3.2m above normal, submerging residential areas and critical infrastructure. 1031 fatalities have been confirmed so far.

HUMANITARIAN NEEDS

- Emergency shelter for 18,334 displaced persons
- Clean drinking water — municipal supply contaminated
- Medical supplies: waterborne disease prevention kits
- Food supplies for an estimated 68,051 affected individuals
- Search and rescue operations in 27 submerged neighborhoods

RESPONSE ACTIONS

National disaster management authority has declared a state of emergency. Military helicopters deployed for evacuation. International aid agencies mobilising emergency response teams. Temporary shelters established at 31 locations including schools and community centers.

RESOURCE REQUIREMENTS

- Water purification units: 78
- Emergency tents: 12,842
- Medical teams: 28
- Rescue boats: 47
- Food ration packs: 184,893

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for epidemic, severity: high, location: Mozambique, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

A measles outbreak has been declared in Mozambique, with 39,103 confirmed cases and 1222 deaths reported across 11 provinces as of today. Case fatality rate stands at 14.1%. The outbreak originated in Maputo and has spread to neighbouring areas.

HEALTH RESPONSE

- 40 treatment centers established
- 3,525 health workers deployed
- Contact tracing: 15,000 contacts identified and monitored
- Vaccination campaign targeting 895,948 people

CHALLENGES

Limited laboratory capacity, inadequate medical supplies, community resistance to health measures, and cross-border movement complicating containment efforts.

RESOURCE NEEDS

WHO has classified this as a Grade 1 emergency. Urgent need for medical supplies, PPE, diagnostic kits, and additional health personnel. Estimated funding gap: $174M.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for drought, severity: critical, location: Somalia, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Prolonged drought conditions in Somalia have entered their 10th consecutive month, with rainfall at 43% of seasonal average. An estimated 3,415,574 people are facing acute food insecurity across 10 regions.

FOOD SECURITY

- IPC Phase 3 (Crisis): 871,186 people
- IPC Phase 4 (Emergency): 131,495 people
- Livestock losses: 259,079 animals
- Crop production: 49% below average

WATER SITUATION

67% of water sources dried up or critically low. Water trucking operations serving 28,755 people daily but demand far exceeds current capacity.

HUMANITARIAN RESPONSE

FAO and WFP scaling up emergency food assistance. Supplementary feeding programmes targeting 51,750 malnourished children under 5. Cash transfer programmes reaching 90,387 households.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for flood, severity: critical, location: Afghanistan, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Heavy monsoon rains have caused severe flooding in Kabul, Afghanistan, displacing approximately 295,925 people across 18 districts. Water levels have risen to 6.0m above normal, submerging residential areas and critical infrastructure. 598 fatalities have been confirmed so far.

HUMANITARIAN NEEDS

- Emergency shelter for 295,925 displaced persons
- Clean drinking water — municipal supply contaminated
- Medical supplies: waterborne disease prevention kits
- Food supplies for an estimated 179,562 affected individuals
- Search and rescue operations in 15 submerged neighborhoods

RESPONSE ACTIONS

National disaster management authority has declared a state of emergency. Military helicopters deployed for evacuation. International aid agencies mobilising emergency response teams. Temporary shelters established at 22 locations including schools and community centers.

RESOURCE REQUIREMENTS

- Water purification units: 152
- Emergency tents: 2,147
- Medical teams: 37
- Rescue boats: 44
- Food ration packs: 273,706

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for cyclone, severity: high, location: Indonesia, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:

Tropical Cyclone Dorian made landfall near Jakarta, Indonesia as a Category 3 storm with sustained winds of 170km/h. Storm surge of 3.5m reported along the coastline. 1233 fatalities confirmed, with 461 persons still missing.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for earthquake, severity: critical, location: Haiti, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

A magnitude 8.0 earthquake struck Port-au-Prince, Haiti at 18:34 local time, with the epicentre located 36km underground. The quake caused widespread structural damage across the metropolitan area. 1033 deaths confirmed, 49,090 injured, and an estimated 441,463 people displaced from their homes.

DAMAGE ASSESSMENT

- 17,717 buildings collapsed or severely damaged
- Critical infrastructure: bridges damaged
- Hospitals operating at reduced capacity
- Power outages affecting 278,212 households
- Road access disrupted in 21 areas

HUMANITARIAN NEEDS

Immediate priorities include urban search and rescue, emergency medical care, temporary shelter, and restoration of water supply. Aftershocks continuing, complicating rescue efforts.

COORDINATION

UN OCHA has activated the cluster system. UNDAC team deployed. International search and rescue teams from 6 countries arriving. Government has requested international assistance.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for drought, severity: critical, location: Philippines, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Prolonged drought conditions in Philippines have entered their 15th consecutive month, with rainfall at 14% of seasonal average. An estimated 2,230,152 people are facing acute food insecurity across 4 regions.

FOOD SECURITY

- IPC Phase 3 (Crisis): 1,663,638 people
- IPC Phase 4 (Emergency): 439,719 people
- Livestock losses: 148,520 animals
- Crop production: 58% below average

WATER SITUATION

46% of water sources dried up or critically low. Water trucking operations serving 165,440 people daily but demand far exceeds current capacity.

HUMANITARIAN RESPONSE

FAO and WFP scaling up emergency food assistance. Supplementary feeding programmes targeting 91,992 malnourished children under 5. Cash transfer programmes reaching 81,823 households.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for epidemic, severity: high, location: Nigeria, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

A typhoid outbreak has been declared in Nigeria, with 22,279 confirmed cases and 683 deaths reported across 3 provinces as of today. Case fatality rate stands at 12.3%. The outbreak originated in Lagos and has spread to neighbouring areas.

HEALTH RESPONSE

- 22 treatment centers established
- 2,328 health workers deployed
- Contact tracing: 8,167 contacts identified and monitored
- Vaccination campaign targeting 1,277,077 people

CHALLENGES

Limited laboratory capacity, inadequate medical supplies, community resistance to health measures, and cross-border movement complicating containment efforts.

RESOURCE NEEDS

WHO has classified this as a Grade 1 emergency. Urgent need for medical supplies, PPE, diagnostic kits, and additional health personnel. Estimated funding gap: $10M.

FOOD SECURITY

- IPC Phase 3 (Crisis): 1,067,493 people
- IPC Phase 4 (Emergency): 230,124 people
- Livestock losses: 378,080 animals
- Crop production: 56% below average

WATER SITUATION

62% of water sources dried up or critically low. Water trucking operations serving 160,288 people daily but demand far exceeds current capacity.

HUMANITARIAN RESPONSE

FAO and WFP scaling up emergency food assistance. Supplementary feeding programmes targeting 122,148 malnourished children under 5. Cash transfer programmes reaching 37,167 households.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for epidemic, severity: high, location: Nigeria, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

A dengue fever outbreak has been declared in Nigeria, with 19,479 confirmed cases and 501 deaths reported across 11 provinces as of today. Case fatality rate stands at 3.7%. The outbreak originated in Lagos and has spread to neighbouring areas.

HEALTH RESPONSE

- 18 treatment centers established
- 4,952 health workers deployed
- Contact tracing: 33,569 contacts identified and monitored
- Vaccination campaign targeting 1,851,688 people

CHALLENGES

Limited laboratory capacity, inadequate medical supplies, community resistance to health measures, and cross-border movement complicating containment efforts.

RESOURCE NEEDS

WHO has classified this as a Grade 1 emergency. Urgent need for medical supplies, PPE, diagnostic kits, and additional health personnel. Estimated funding gap: $199M.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for flood, severity: critical, location: Philippines, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Heavy monsoon rains have caused severe flooding in Manila, Philippines, displacing approximately 275,531 people across 14 districts. Water levels have risen to 6.6m above normal, submerging residential areas and critical infrastructure. 608 fatalities have been confirmed so far.

HUMANITARIAN NEEDS

- Emergency shelter for 275,531 displaced persons
- Clean drinking water — municipal supply contaminated
- Medical supplies: waterborne disease prevention kits
- Food supplies for an estimated 181,124 affected individuals
- Search and rescue operations in 21 submerged neighborhoods

RESPONSE ACTIONS

National disaster management authority has declared a state of emergency. Military helicopters deployed for evacuation. International aid agencies mobilising emergency response teams. Temporary shelters established at 29 locations including schools and community centers.

RESOURCE REQUIREMENTS

- Water purification units: 52
- Emergency tents: 2,984
- Medical teams: 24
- Rescue boats: 56
- Food ration packs: 210,768

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for cyclone, severity: high, location: Pakistan, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Tropical Cyclone Irma made landfall near Karachi, Pakistan as a Category 2 storm with sustained winds of 232km/h. Storm surge of 3.5m reported along the coastline. 1963 fatalities confirmed, with 320 persons still missing.

IMPACT

- 265,165 people evacuated from coastal communities
- Extensive damage to crops affecting 45,971 hectares
- Fishing fleet: 1685 vessels damaged or destroyed
- Communication networks disrupted across 15 provinces

RESPONSE PRIORITIES

1. Search and rescue in affected coastal communities
2. Emergency shelter and food distribution
3. Restoration of clean water supply
4. Medical response — injury treatment and disease prevention
5. Debris clearance for road access

RESOURCE MOBILIZATION

Red Cross/Red Crescent has deployed 11 emergency response units. WFP pre-positioned stocks being distributed. Government allocated emergency fund of $304M for immediate relief operations.

- Vaccination campaign targeting 1,413,833 people

Limited laboratory capacity, inadequate medical supplies, community resistance to health measures, and cross-border movement complicating containment efforts.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for cyclone, severity: high, location: Nepal, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Tropical Cyclone Dorian made landfall near Kathmandu, Nepal as a Category 4 storm with sustained winds of 145km/h. Storm surge of 2.5m reported along the coastline. 1604 fatalities confirmed, with 477 persons still missing.

IMPACT

- 191,012 people evacuated from coastal communities
- Extensive damage to crops affecting 73,228 hectares
- Fishing fleet: 1017 vessels damaged or destroyed
- Communication networks disrupted across 6 provinces

RESPONSE PRIORITIES

1. Search and rescue in affected coastal communities
2. Emergency shelter and food distribution
3. Restoration of clean water supply
4. Medical response — injury treatment and disease prevention
5. Debris clearance for road access

RESOURCE MOBILIZATION

Red Cross/Red Crescent has deployed 23 emergency response units. WFP pre-positioned stocks being distributed. Government allocated emergency fund of $374M for immediate relief operations.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for earthquake, severity: critical, location: Somalia, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

A magnitude 8.0 earthquake struck Mogadishu, Somalia at 22:42 local time, with the epicentre located 75km underground. The quake caused widespread structural damage across the metropolitan area. 521 deaths confirmed, 17,678 injured, and an estimated 70,311 people displaced from their homes.

DAMAGE ASSESSMENT

- 4,476 buildings collapsed or severely damaged
- Critical infrastructure: water main ruptured
- Hospitals operating at reduced capacity
- Power outages affecting 261,847 households
- Road access disrupted in 24 areas

HUMANITARIAN NEEDS

Immediate priorities include urban search and rescue, emergency medical care, temporary shelter, and restoration of water supply. Aftershocks continuing, complicating rescue efforts.

COORDINATION

UN OCHA has activated the cluster system. UNDAC team deployed. International search and rescue teams from 5 countries arriving. Government has requested international assistance.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for drought, severity: critical, location: Haiti, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Prolonged drought conditions in Haiti have entered their 11th consecutive month, with rainfall at 29% of seasonal average. An estimated 572,449 people are facing acute food insecurity across 9 regions.

FOOD SECURITY

- IPC Phase 3 (Crisis): 1,813,865 people
- IPC Phase 4 (Emergency): 275,390 people
- Livestock losses: 483,911 animals
- Crop production: 70% below average

WATER SITUATION

46% of water sources dried up or critically low. Water trucking operations serving 106,690 people daily but demand far exceeds current capacity.

HUMANITARIAN RESPONSE

FAO and WFP scaling up emergency food assistance. Supplementary feeding programmes targeting 20,112 malnourished children under 5. Cash transfer programmes reaching 93,030 households.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for epidemic, severity: high, location: Japan, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

A measles outbreak has been declared in Japan, with 46,592 confirmed cases and 54 deaths reported across 16 provinces as of today. Case fatality rate stands at 22.4%. The outbreak originated in Tokyo and has spread to neighbouring areas.

HEALTH RESPONSE

- 13 treatment centers established
- 2,938 health workers deployed
- Contact tracing: 14,750 contacts identified and monitored
- Vaccination campaign targeting 1,113,580 people

CHALLENGES

Limited laboratory capacity, inadequate medical supplies, community resistance to health measures, and cross-border movement complicating containment efforts.

RESOURCE NEEDS

WHO has classified this as a Grade 3 emergency. Urgent need for medical supplies, PPE, diagnostic kits, and additional health personnel. Estimated funding gap: $13M.

A magnitude 7.7 earthquake struck Lagos, Nigeria at 01:25 local time, with the epicentre located 8km underground. The quake caused widespread structural damage across the metropolitan area. 1926 deaths confirmed, 13,132 injured, and an estimated 314,263 people displaced from their homes.

DAMAGE ASSESSMENT

- 7,748 buildings collapsed or severely damaged
- Critical infrastructure: airport runway cracked
- Hospitals operating at reduced capacity
- Power outages affecting 12,397 households
- Road access disrupted in 34 areas

HUMANITARIAN NEEDS

Immediate priorities include urban search and rescue, emergency medical care, temporary shelter, and restoration of water supply. Aftershocks continuing, complicating rescue efforts.

COORDINATION

UN OCHA has activated the cluster system. UNDAC team deployed. International search and rescue teams from 13 countries arriving. Government has requested international assistance.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for drought, severity: critical, location: Pakistan, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Prolonged drought conditions in Pakistan have entered their 7th consecutive month, with rainfall at 14% of seasonal average. An estimated 359,552 people are facing acute food insecurity across 11 regions.

FOOD SECURITY

- IPC Phase 3 (Crisis): 1,423,831 people
- IPC Phase 4 (Emergency): 474,910 people
- Livestock losses: 254,237 animals
- Crop production: 79% below average

WATER SITUATION

63% of water sources dried up or critically low. Water trucking operations serving 29,042 people daily but demand far exceeds current capacity.

HUMANITARIAN RESPONSE

FAO and WFP scaling up emergency food assistance. Supplementary feeding programmes targeting 133,025 malnourished children under 5. Cash transfer programmes reaching 92,919 households.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for flood, severity: critical, location: Nigeria, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Heavy monsoon rains have caused severe flooding in Lagos, Nigeria, displacing approximately 185,381 people across 13 districts. Water levels have risen to 5.1m above normal, submerging residential areas and critical infrastructure. 1254 fatalities have been confirmed so far.

HUMANITARIAN NEEDS

- Emergency shelter for 185,381 displaced persons
- Clean drinking water — municipal supply contaminated
- Medical supplies: waterborne disease prevention kits
- Food supplies for an estimated 78,974 affected individuals
- Search and rescue operations in 8 submerged neighborhoods

RESPONSE ACTIONS

National disaster management authority has declared a state of emergency. Military helicopters deployed for evacuation. International aid agencies mobilising emergency response teams. Temporary shelters established at 92 locations including schools and community centers.

RESOURCE REQUIREMENTS

- Water purification units: 154
- Emergency tents: 15,518
- Medical teams: 47
- Rescue boats: 115
- Food ration packs: 66,200

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for cyclone, severity: high, location: Bangladesh, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Tropical Cyclone Winston made landfall near Dhaka, Bangladesh as a Category 4 storm with sustained winds of 136km/h. Storm surge of 3.5m reported along the coastline. 996 fatalities confirmed, with 169 persons still missing.

IMPACT

- 456,399 people evacuated from coastal communities
- Extensive damage to crops affecting 21,888 hectares
- Fishing fleet: 637 vessels damaged or destroyed
- Communication networks disrupted across 13 provinces

RESPONSE PRIORITIES

1. Search and rescue in affected coastal communities
2. Emergency shelter and food distribution
3. Restoration of clean water supply
4. Medical response — injury treatment and disease prevention
5. Debris clearance for road access

RESOURCE MOBILIZATION

Red Cross/Red Crescent has deployed 6 emergency response units. WFP pre-positioned stocks being distributed. Government allocated emergency fund of $376M for immediate relief operations.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for epidemic, severity: high, location: Ethiopia, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

A malaria outbreak has been declared in Ethiopia, with 46,393 confirmed cases and 57 deaths reported across 5 provinces as of today. Case fatality rate stands at 12.3%. The outbreak originated in Addis Ababa and has spread to neighbouring areas.

HEALTH RESPONSE

- 5 treatment centers established
- 2,788 health workers deployed
- Contact tracing: 46,599 contacts identified and monitored
- Vaccination campaign targeting 522,442 people

CHALLENGES

Limited laboratory capacity, inadequate medical supplies, community resistance to health measures, and cross-border movement complicating containment efforts.

RESOURCE NEEDS

WHO has classified this as a Grade 2 emergency. Urgent need for medical supplies, PPE, diagnostic kits, and additional health personnel. Estimated funding gap: $156M.

A magnitude 6.5 earthquake struck Addis Ababa, Ethiopia at 01:48 local time, with the epicentre located 68km underground. The quake caused widespread structural damage across the metropolitan area. 305 deaths confirmed, 16,493 injured, and an estimated 8,518 people displaced from their homes.

DAMAGE ASSESSMENT

- 11,668 buildings collapsed or severely damaged
- Critical infrastructure: water main ruptured
- Hospitals operating at reduced capacity
- Power outages affecting 299,511 households
- Road access disrupted in 11 areas

HUMANITARIAN NEEDS

Immediate priorities include urban search and rescue, emergency medical care, temporary shelter, and restoration of water supply. Aftershocks continuing, complicating rescue efforts.

COORDINATION

UN OCHA has activated the cluster system. UNDAC team deployed. International search and rescue teams from 6 countries arriving. Government has requested international assistance.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for cyclone, severity: high, location: Afghanistan, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Tropical Cyclone Irma made landfall near Kabul, Afghanistan as a Category 2 storm with sustained winds of 228km/h. Storm surge of 3.5m reported along the coastline. 914 fatalities confirmed, with 358 persons still missing.

IMPACT

- 365,051 people evacuated from coastal communities
- Extensive damage to crops affecting 172,284 hectares
- Fishing fleet: 560 vessels damaged or destroyed
- Communication networks disrupted across 14 provinces

RESPONSE PRIORITIES

1. Search and rescue in affected coastal communities
2. Emergency shelter and food distribution
3. Restoration of clean water supply
4. Medical response — injury treatment and disease prevention
5. Debris clearance for road access

RESOURCE MOBILIZATION

Red Cross/Red Crescent has deployed 6 emergency response units. WFP pre-positioned stocks being distributed. Government allocated emergency fund of $169M for immediate relief operations.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for flood, severity: critical, location: Somalia, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Heavy monsoon rains have caused severe flooding in Mogadishu, Somalia, displacing approximately 302,582 people across 17 districts. Water levels have risen to 7.0m above normal, submerging residential areas and critical infrastructure. 423 fatalities have been confirmed so far.

HUMANITARIAN NEEDS

- Emergency shelter for 302,582 displaced persons
- Clean drinking water — municipal supply contaminated
- Medical supplies: waterborne disease prevention kits

- Search and rescue operations in 24 submerged neighborhoods

National disaster management authority has declared a state of emergency. Military helicopters deployed for evacuation. International aid agencies mobilising emergency response teams. Temporary shelters established at 12 locations including schools and community centers.

RESOURCE REQUIREMENTS

- Water purification units: 188
- Emergency tents: 12,062
- Medical teams: 39
- Rescue boats: 158
- Food ration packs: 383,196

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for epidemic, severity: high, location: India, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

A measles outbreak has been declared in India, with 727 confirmed cases and 1477 deaths reported across 12 provinces as of today. Case fatality rate stands at 18.1%. The outbreak originated in Mumbai and has spread to neighbouring areas.

HEALTH RESPONSE

- 9 treatment centers established
- 2,947 health workers deployed
- Contact tracing: 10,174 contacts identified and monitored
- Vaccination campaign targeting 1,564,827 people

CHALLENGES

Limited laboratory capacity, inadequate medical supplies, community resistance to health measures, and cross-border movement complicating containment efforts.

RESOURCE NEEDS

WHO has classified this as a Grade 2 emergency. Urgent need for medical supplies, PPE, diagnostic kits, and additional health personnel. Estimated funding gap: $38M.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for drought, severity: critical, location: Afghanistan, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Prolonged drought conditions in Afghanistan have entered their 17th consecutive month, with rainfall at 33% of seasonal average. An estimated 3,538,860 people are facing acute food insecurity across 5 regions.

FOOD SECURITY

- IPC Phase 3 (Crisis): 568,967 people
- IPC Phase 4 (Emergency): 130,649 people
- Livestock losses: 348,408 animals
- Crop production: 74% below average

WATER SITUATION

60% of water sources dried up or critically low. Water trucking operations serving 138,023 people daily but demand far exceeds current capacity.

HUMANITARIAN RESPONSE

FAO and WFP scaling up emergency food assistance. Supplementary feeding programmes targeting 14,579 malnourished children under 5. Cash transfer programmes reaching 32,339 households.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for cyclone, severity: high, location: Nigeria, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Tropical Cyclone Fani made landfall near Lagos, Nigeria as a Category 4 storm with sustained winds of 137km/h. Storm surge of 2.4m reported along the coastline. 1131 fatalities confirmed, with 120 persons still missing.

IMPACT

- 29,478 people evacuated from coastal communities
- Extensive damage to crops affecting 136,484 hectares
- Fishing fleet: 1232 vessels damaged or destroyed
- Communication networks disrupted across 9 provinces

RESPONSE PRIORITIES

Red Cross/Red Crescent has deployed 15 emergency response units. WFP pre-positioned stocks being distributed. Government allocated emergency fund of $357M for immediate relief operations.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for flood, severity: critical, location: Syria, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Heavy monsoon rains have caused severe flooding in Aleppo, Syria, displacing approximately 225,344 people across 3 districts. Water levels have risen to 6.5m above normal, submerging residential areas and critical infrastructure. 1749 fatalities have been confirmed so far.

HUMANITARIAN NEEDS

- Emergency shelter for 225,344 displaced persons
- Clean drinking water — municipal supply contaminated
- Medical supplies: waterborne disease prevention kits
- Food supplies for an estimated 37,725 affected individuals
- Search and rescue operations in 21 submerged neighborhoods

RESPONSE ACTIONS

National disaster management authority has declared a state of emergency. Military helicopters deployed for evacuation. International aid agencies mobilising emergency response teams. Temporary shelters established at 96 locations including schools and community centers.

RESOURCE REQUIREMENTS

- Water purification units: 97
- Emergency tents: 17,394
- Medical teams: 25
- Rescue boats: 32
- Food ration packs: 214,175

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for epidemic, severity: high, location: Kenya, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

A malaria outbreak has been declared in Kenya, with 37,183 confirmed cases and 446 deaths reported across 11 provinces as of today. Case fatality rate stands at 23.6%. The outbreak originated in Nairobi and has spread to neighbouring areas.

HEALTH RESPONSE

- 7 treatment centers established
- 2,358 health workers deployed
- Contact tracing: 8,553 contacts identified and monitored
- Vaccination campaign targeting 647,808 people

CHALLENGES

Limited laboratory capacity, inadequate medical supplies, community resistance to health measures, and cross-border movement complicating containment efforts.

RESOURCE NEEDS

WHO has classified this as a Grade 2 emergency. Urgent need for medical supplies, PPE, diagnostic kits, and additional health personnel. Estimated funding gap: $29M.
