records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for drought, severity: critical, location: Philippines, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Prolonged drought conditions in Philippines have entered their 11th consecutive month, with rainfall at 29% of seasonal average. An estimated 319,941 people are facing acute food insecurity across 3 regions.

FOOD SECURITY

- IPC Phase 3 (Crisis): 1,076,400 people
- IPC Phase 4 (Emergency): 473,457 people
- Livestock losses: 258,035 animals
- Crop production: 74% below average

WATER SITUATION

66% of water sources dried up or critically low. Water trucking operations serving 172,411 people daily but demand far exceeds current capacity.

HUMANITARIAN RESPONSE

FAO and WFP scaling up emergency food assistance. Supplementary feeding programmes targeting 161,970 malnourished children under 5. Cash transfer programmes reaching 42,786 households.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for epidemic, severity: high, location: Afghanistan, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

A dengue fever outbreak has been declared in Afghanistan, with 13,637 confirmed cases and 950 deaths reported across 15 provinces as of today. Case fatality rate stands at 1.5%. The outbreak originated in Kabul and has spread to neighbouring areas.

HEALTH RESPONSE

- 28 treatment centers established
- 4,154 health workers deployed
- Contact tracing: 26,955 contacts identified and monitored
- Vaccination campaign targeting 394,322 people

CHALLENGES

Limited laboratory capacity, inadequate medical supplies, community resistance to health measures, and cross-border movement complicating containment efforts.

RESOURCE NEEDS

WHO has classified this as a Grade 2 emergency. Urgent need for medical supplies, PPE, diagnostic kits, and additional health personnel. Estimated funding gap: $37M.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for flood, severity: critical, location: India, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Heavy monsoon rains have caused severe flooding in Mumbai, India, displacing approximately 271,545 people across 18 districts. Water levels have risen to 3.9m above normal, submerging residential areas and critical infrastructure. 1482 fatalities have been confirmed so far.

HUMANITARIAN NEEDS

- Emergency shelter for 271,545 displaced persons
- Clean drinking water — municipal supply contaminated
- Medical supplies: waterborne disease prevention kits
- Food supplies for an estimated 196,340 affected individuals
- Search and rescue operations in 16 submerged neighborhoods

RESPONSE ACTIONS

National disaster management authority has declared a state of emergency. Military helicopters deployed for evacuation. International aid agencies mobilising emergency response teams. Temporary shelters established at 19 locations including schools and community centers.

RESOURCE REQUIREMENTS

- Water purification units: 27
- Emergency tents: 941
- Medical teams: 6
- Rescue boats: 96
- Food ration packs: 347,517

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for earthquake, severity: critical, location: India, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

A magnitude 7.5 earthquake struck Mumbai, India at 02:26 local time, with the epicentre located 23km underground. The quake caused widespread structural damage across the metropolitan area. 831 deaths confirmed, 45,636 injured, and an estimated 439,292 people displaced from their homes.

DAMAGE ASSESSMENT

- 21,231 buildings collapsed or severely damaged
- Critical infrastructure: water main ruptured
- Hospitals operating at reduced capacity
- Power outages affecting 353,534 households
- Road access disrupted in 34 areas

HUMANITARIAN NEEDS

Immediate priorities include urban search and rescue, emergency medical care, temporary shelter, and restoration of water supply. Aftershocks continuing, complicating rescue efforts.

COORDINATION

UN OCHA has activated the cluster system. UNDAC team deployed. International search and rescue teams from 13 countries arriving. Government has requested international assistance.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for cyclone, severity: high, location: Indonesia, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Tropical Cyclone Fani made landfall near Jakarta, Indonesia as a Category 4 storm with sustained winds of 231km/h. Storm surge of 7.9m reported along the coastline. 423 fatalities confirmed, with 188 persons still missing.

IMPACT

- 168,728 people evacuated from coastal communities
- Extensive damage to crops affecting 140,395 hectares
- Fishing fleet: 464 vessels damaged or destroyed
- Communication networks disrupted across 6 provinces

RESPONSE PRIORITIES

1. Search and rescue in affected coastal communities
2. Emergency shelter and food distribution
3. Restoration of clean water supply
4. Medical response — injury treatment and disease prevention
5. Debris clearance for road access

RESOURCE MOBILIZATION

Red Cross/Red Crescent has deployed 30 emergency response units. WFP pre-positioned stocks being distributed. Government allocated emergency fund of $121M for immediate relief operations.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for drought, severity: critical, location: Turkey, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Prolonged drought conditions in Turkey have entered their 13th consecutive month, with rainfall at 36% of seasonal average. An estimated 856,487 people are facing acute food insecurity across 6 regions.

FOOD SECURITY

- IPC Phase 3 (Crisis): 1,860,994 people
- IPC Phase 4 (Emergency): 218,522 people
- Livestock losses: 463,649 animals
- Crop production: 58% below average

WATER SITUATION

FAO and WFP scaling up emergency food assistance. Supplementary feeding programmes targeting 191,607 malnourished children under 5. Cash transfer programmes reaching 25,738 households.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for earthquake, severity: critical, location: Pakistan, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

A magnitude 6.4 earthquake struck Karachi, Pakistan at 16:38 local time, with the epicentre located 68km underground. The quake caused widespread structural damage across the metropolitan area. 448 deaths confirmed, 45,059 injured, and an estimated 308,090 people displaced from their homes.

DAMAGE ASSESSMENT

- 921 buildings collapsed or severely damaged
- Critical infrastructure: bridges damaged
- Hospitals operating at reduced capacity
- Power outages affecting 52,344 households
- Road access disrupted in 12 areas

HUMANITARIAN NEEDS

Immediate priorities include urban search and rescue, emergency medical care, temporary shelter, and restoration of water supply. Aftershocks continuing, complicating rescue efforts.

COORDINATION

UN OCHA has activated the cluster system. UNDAC team deployed. International search and rescue teams from 9 countries arriving. Government has requested international assistance.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for flood, severity: critical, location: India, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Heavy monsoon rains have caused severe flooding in Mumbai, India, displacing approximately 152,358 people across 20 districts. Water levels have risen to 6.2m above normal, submerging residential areas and critical infrastructure. 1123 fatalities have been confirmed so far.

HUMANITARIAN NEEDS

- Emergency shelter for 152,358 displaced persons
- Clean drinking water — municipal supply contaminated
- Medical supplies: waterborne disease prevention kits
- Food supplies for an estimated 55,032 affected individuals
- Search and rescue operations in 14 submerged neighborhoods

RESPONSE ACTIONS

National disaster management authority has declared a state of emergency. Military helicopters deployed for evacuation. International aid agencies mobilising emergency response teams. Temporary shelters established at 63 locations including schools and community centers.

RESOURCE REQUIREMENTS

- Water purification units: 107
- Emergency tents: 2,249
- Medical teams: 41
- Rescue boats: 107
- Food ration packs: 19,852

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for epidemic, severity: high, location: Japan, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

A measles outbreak has been declared in Japan, with 44,906 confirmed cases and 572 deaths reported across 7 provinces as of today. Case fatality rate stands at 17.3%. The outbreak originated in Tokyo and has spread to neighbouring areas.

HEALTH RESPONSE

- 6 treatment centers established
- 1,966 health workers deployed
- Contact tracing: 32,432 contacts identified and monitored
- Vaccination campaign targeting 1,108,315 people

CHALLENGES

Limited laboratory capacity, inadequate medical supplies, community resistance to health measures, and cross-border movement complicating containment efforts.

RESOURCE NEEDS

WHO has classified this as a Grade 2 emergency. Urgent need for medical supplies, PPE, diagnostic kits, and additional health personnel. Estimated funding gap: $159M.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for flood, severity: critical, location: Haiti, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Heavy monsoon rains have caused severe flooding in Port-au-Prince, Haiti, displacing approximately 385,659 people across 6 districts. Water levels have risen to 2.6m above normal, submerging residential areas and critical infrastructure. 1210 fatalities have been confirmed so far.

HUMANITARIAN NEEDS

- Emergency shelter for 385,659 displaced persons
- Clean drinking water — municipal supply contaminated
- Medical supplies: waterborne disease prevention kits
- Food supplies for an estimated 145,221 affected individuals
- Search and rescue operations in 15 submerged neighborhoods

RESPONSE ACTIONS

National disaster management authority has declared a state of emergency. Military helicopters deployed for evacuation. International aid agencies mobilising emergency response teams. Temporary shelters established at 37 locations including schools and community centers.

RESOURCE REQUIREMENTS

- Water purification units: 192
- Emergency tents: 19,346
- Medical teams: 12
- Rescue boats: 66
- Food ration packs: 297,006

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for drought, severity: critical, location: Nigeria, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Prolonged drought conditions in Nigeria have entered their 18th consecutive month, with rainfall at 39% of seasonal average. An estimated 4,177,608 people are facing acute food insecurity across 5 regions.

FOOD SECURITY

- IPC Phase 3 (Crisis): 1,161,307 people
- IPC Phase 4 (Emergency): 395,476 people
- Livestock losses: 253,959 animals
- Crop production: 38% below average

WATER SITUATION

45% of water sources dried up or critically low. Water trucking operations serving 110,627 people daily but demand far exceeds current capacity.

HUMANITARIAN RESPONSE

FAO and WFP scaling up emergency food assistance. Supplementary feeding programmes targeting 129,925 malnourished children under 5. Cash transfer programmes reaching 74,273 households.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for cyclone, severity: high, location: Somalia, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Tropical Cyclone Amphan made landfall near Mogadishu, Somalia as a Category 5 storm with sustained winds of 187km/h. Storm surge of 6.5m reported along the coastline. 1288 fatalities confirmed, with 244 persons still missing.

IMPACT

- 242,295 people evacuated from coastal communities
- Extensive damage to crops affecting 146,963 hectares
- Fishing fleet: 858 vessels damaged or destroyed
- Communication networks disrupted across 10 provinces

RESPONSE PRIORITIES

1. Search and rescue in affected coastal communities
2. Emergency shelter and food distribution
3. Restoration of clean water supply
4. Medical response — injury treatment and disease prevention
5. Debris clearance for road access

RESOURCE MOBILIZATION

Red Cross/Red Crescent has deployed 25 emergency response units. WFP pre-positioned stocks being distributed. Government allocated emergency fund of $173M for immediate relief operations.

records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Tropical Cyclone Amphan made landfall near Istanbul, Turkey as a Category 2 storm with sustained winds of 195km/h. Storm surge of 3.0m reported along the coastline. 1432 fatalities confirmed, with 336 persons still missing.

IMPACT

- 89,290 people evacuated from coastal communities
- Extensive damage to crops affecting 56,531 hectares
- Fishing fleet: 776 vessels damaged or destroyed
- Communication networks disrupted across 7 provinces

RESPONSE PRIORITIES

1. Search and rescue in affected coastal communities
2. Emergency shelter and food distribution
3. Restoration of clean water supply
4. Medical response — injury treatment and disease prevention
5. Debris clearance for road access

RESOURCE MOBILIZATION

Red Cross/Red Crescent has deployed 19 emergency response units. WFP pre-positioned stocks being distributed. Government allocated emergency fund of $224M for immediate relief operations.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for epidemic, severity: high, location: Somalia, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

A typhoid outbreak has been declared in Somalia, with 7,890 confirmed cases and 1611 deaths reported across 4 provinces as of today. Case fatality rate stands at 24.0%. The outbreak originated in Mogadishu and has spread to neighbouring areas.

HEALTH RESPONSE

- 39 treatment centers established
- 888 health workers deployed
- Contact tracing: 21,495 contacts identified and monitored
- Vaccination campaign targeting 1,282,815 people

CHALLENGES

Limited laboratory capacity, inadequate medical supplies, community resistance to health measures, and cross-border movement complicating containment efforts.

RESOURCE NEEDS

WHO has classified this as a Grade 1 emergency. Urgent need for medical supplies, PPE, diagnostic kits, and additional health personnel. Estimated funding gap: $148M.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for drought, severity: critical, location: Turkey, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Prolonged drought conditions in Turkey have entered their 9th consecutive month, with rainfall at 39% of seasonal average. An estimated 969,666 people are facing acute food insecurity across 10 regions.

FOOD SECURITY

- IPC Phase 3 (Crisis): 647,017 people
- IPC Phase 4 (Emergency): 381,802 people
- Livestock losses: 293,677 animals
- Crop production: 58% below average

WATER SITUATION

57% of water sources dried up or critically low. Water trucking operations serving 26,546 people daily but demand far exceeds current capacity.

HUMANITARIAN RESPONSE

FAO and WFP scaling up emergency food assistance. Supplementary feeding programmes targeting 75,346 malnourished children under 5. Cash transfer programmes reaching 9,343 households.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for epidemic, severity: high, location: Afghanistan, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

A malaria outbreak has been declared in Afghanistan, with 48,711 confirmed cases and 1923 deaths reported across 18 provinces as of today. Case fatality rate stands at 17.8%. The outbreak originated in Kabul and has spread to neighbouring areas.

HEALTH RESPONSE

- 19 treatment centers established
- 4,174 health workers deployed
- Contact tracing: 3,287 contacts identified and monitored
- Vaccination campaign targeting 1,634,991 people

CHALLENGES

Limited laboratory capacity, inadequate medical supplies, community resistance to health measures, and cross-border movement complicating containment efforts.

RESOURCE NEEDS

WHO has classified this as a Grade 2 emergency. Urgent need for medical supplies, PPE, diagnostic kits, and additional health personnel. Estimated funding gap: $95M.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for drought, severity: critical, location: Nepal, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Prolonged drought conditions in Nepal have entered their 5th consecutive month, with rainfall at 42% of seasonal average. An estimated 4,874,831 people are facing acute food insecurity across 12 regions.

FOOD SECURITY

- IPC Phase 3 (Crisis): 962,369 people
- IPC Phase 4 (Emergency): 273,033 people
- Livestock losses: 233,256 animals
- Crop production: 44% below average

WATER SITUATION

51% of water sources dried up or critically low. Water trucking operations serving 119,499 people daily but demand far exceeds current capacity.

HUMANITARIAN RESPONSE

FAO and WFP scaling up emergency food assistance. Supplementary feeding programmes targeting 61,775 malnourished children under 5. Cash transfer programmes reaching 19,673 households.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for flood, severity: critical, location: Nepal, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Heavy monsoon rains have caused severe flooding in Kathmandu, Nepal, displacing approximately 214,563 people across 4 districts. Water levels have risen to 7.1m above normal, submerging residential areas and critical infrastructure. 1258 fatalities have been confirmed so far.

HUMANITARIAN NEEDS

- Emergency shelter for 214,563 displaced persons
- Clean drinking water — municipal supply contaminated
- Medical supplies: waterborne disease prevention kits
- Food supplies for an estimated 132,518 affected individuals
- Search and rescue operations in 27 submerged neighborhoods

RESPONSE ACTIONS

National disaster management authority has declared a state of emergency. Military helicopters deployed for evacuation. International aid agencies mobilising emergency response teams. Temporary shelters established at 63 locations including schools and community centers.

RESOURCE REQUIREMENTS

- Water purification units: 108
- Emergency tents: 5,842
- Medical teams: 33
- Rescue boats: 70
- Food ration packs: 217,543

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for earthquake, severity: critical, location: Turkey, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

A magnitude 7.4 earthquake struck Istanbul, Turkey at 09:46 local time, with the epicentre located 8km underground. The quake caused widespread structural damage across the metropolitan area. 1418 deaths confirmed, 4,154 injured, and an estimated 228,870 people displaced from their homes.

DAMAGE ASSESSMENT

- 23,757 buildings collapsed or severely damaged
- Critical infrastructure: water main ruptured
- Hospitals operating at reduced capacity
- Power outages affecting 420,821 households
- Road access disrupted in 38 areas

HUMANITARIAN NEEDS

Immediate priorities include urban search and rescue, emergency medical care, temporary shelter, and restoration of water supply. Aftershocks continuing, complicating rescue efforts.

COORDINATION

UN OCHA has activated the cluster system. UNDAC team deployed. International search and rescue teams from 3 countries arriving. Government has requested international assistance.

- Critical infrastructure: water main ruptured
- Hospitals operating at reduced capacity
- Power outages affecting 420,821 households
- Road access disrupted in 38 areas

HUMANITARIAN NEEDS

Immediate priorities include urban search and rescue, emergency medical care, temporary shelter, and restoration of water supply. Aftershocks continuing, complicating rescue efforts.

UN OCHA has activated the cluster system. UNDAC team deployed. International search and rescue teams from 3 countries arriving. Government has requested international assistance.

Prolonged drought conditions in Japan have entered their 14th consecutive month, with rainfall at 33% of seasonal average. An estimated 270,396 people are facing acute food insecurity across 8 regions.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for cyclone, severity: high, location: Mozambique, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Tropical Cyclone Haiyan made landfall near Maputo, Mozambique as a Category 5 storm with sustained winds of 200km/h. Storm surge of 7.9m reported along the coastline. 273 fatalities confirmed, with 134 persons still missing.

IMPACT

- 118,736 people evacuated from coastal communities
- Extensive damage to crops affecting 137,992 hectares
- Fishing fleet: 262 vessels damaged or destroyed
- Communication networks disrupted across 5 provinces

RESPONSE PRIORITIES

1. Search and rescue in affected coastal communities
2. Emergency shelter and food distribution
3. Restoration of clean water supply
4. Medical response — injury treatment and disease prevention
5. Debris clearance for road access

RESOURCE MOBILIZATION

Red Cross/Red Crescent has deployed 10 emergency response units. WFP pre-positioned stocks being distributed. Government allocated emergency fund of $191M for immediate relief operations.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for epidemic, severity: high, location: Turkey, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

A cholera outbreak has been declared in Turkey, with 38,594 confirmed cases and 471 deaths reported across 13 provinces as of today. Case fatality rate stands at 13.6%. The outbreak originated in Istanbul and has spread to neighbouring areas.

HEALTH RESPONSE

- 34 treatment centers established
- 3,819 health workers deployed
- Contact tracing: 39,184 contacts identified and monitored
- Vaccination campaign targeting 252,241 people

CHALLENGES

Limited laboratory capacity, inadequate medical supplies, community resistance to health measures, and cross-border movement complicating containment efforts.

RESOURCE NEEDS

WHO has classified this as a Grade 2 emergency. Urgent need for medical supplies, PPE, diagnostic kits, and additional health personnel. Estimated funding gap: $61M.

SITUATION OVERVIEW

Prolonged drought conditions in Bangladesh have entered their 6th consecutive month, with rainfall at 29% of seasonal average. An estimated 4,963,189 people are facing acute food insecurity across 6 regions.

FOOD SECURITY

- IPC Phase 3 (Crisis): 1,555,779 people
- IPC Phase 4 (Emergency): 183,281 people
- Livestock losses: 224,155 animals
- Crop production: 77% below average

WATER SITUATION

69% of water sources dried up or critically low. Water trucking operations serving 72,947 people daily but demand far exceeds current capacity.

HUMANITARIAN RESPONSE

FAO and WFP scaling up emergency food assistance. Supplementary feeding programmes targeting 116,501 malnourished children under 5. Cash transfer programmes reaching 45,842 households.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for flood, severity: critical, location: Bangladesh, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Heavy monsoon rains have caused severe flooding in Dhaka, Bangladesh, displacing approximately 13,876 people across 3 districts. Water levels have risen to 3.2m above normal, submerging residential areas and critical infrastructure. 1091 fatalities have been confirmed so far.

HUMANITARIAN NEEDS

- Emergency shelter for 13,876 displaced persons
- Clean drinking water — municipal supply contaminated
- Medical supplies: waterborne disease prevention kits
- Food supplies for an estimated 120,523 affected individuals
- Search and rescue operations in 28 submerged neighborhoods

RESPONSE ACTIONS

National disaster management authority has declared a state of emergency. Military helicopters deployed for evacuation. International aid agencies mobilising emergency response teams. Temporary shelters established at 50 locations including schools and community centers.

RESOURCE REQUIREMENTS

- Water purification units: 54
- Emergency tents: 15,938
- Medical teams: 32
- Rescue boats: 200
- Food ration packs: 212,778

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for epidemic, severity: high, location: Japan, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

A typhoid outbreak has been declared in Japan, with 21,856 confirmed cases and 95 deaths reported across 8 provinces as of today. Case fatality rate stands at 7.8%. The outbreak originated in Tokyo and has spread to neighbouring areas.

HEALTH RESPONSE

- 48 treatment centers established
- 3,321 health workers deployed
- Contact tracing: 42,816 contacts identified and monitored
- Vaccination campaign targeting 1,878,325 people

CHALLENGES

Limited laboratory capacity, inadequate medical supplies, community resistance to health measures, and cross-border movement complicating containment efforts.

RESOURCE NEEDS

WHO has classified this as a Grade 1 emergency. Urgent need for medical supplies, PPE, diagnostic kits, and additional health personnel. Estimated funding gap: $156M.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for earthquake, severity: critical, location: Mozambique, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

A magnitude 5.6 earthquake struck Maputo, Mozambique at 05:56 local time, with the epicentre located 29km underground. The quake caused widespread structural damage across the metropolitan area. 622 deaths confirmed, 7,754 injured, and an estimated 221,625 people displaced from their homes.

DAMAGE ASSESSMENT

- 2,075 buildings collapsed or severely damaged
- Critical infrastructure: water main ruptured
- Hospitals operating at reduced capacity
- Power outages affecting 251,586 households
- Road access disrupted in 40 areas

HUMANITARIAN NEEDS

Immediate priorities include urban search and rescue, emergency medical care, temporary shelter, and restoration of water supply. Aftershocks continuing, complicating rescue efforts.

COORDINATION

UN OCHA has activated the cluster system. UNDAC team deployed. International search and rescue teams from 11 countries arriving. Government has requested international assistance.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for cyclone, severity: high, location: India, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Tropical Cyclone Irma made landfall near Mumbai, India as a Category 2 storm with sustained winds of 129km/h. Storm surge of 7.7m reported along the coastline. 1444 fatalities confirmed, with 23 persons still missing.

IMPACT

- 170,204 people evacuated from coastal communities
- Extensive damage to crops affecting 103,925 hectares
- Fishing fleet: 616 vessels damaged or destroyed
- Communication networks disrupted across 14 provinces

RESPONSE PRIORITIES

1. Search and rescue in affected coastal communities
2. Emergency shelter and food distribution
3. Restoration of clean water supply
4. Medical response — injury treatment and disease prevention
5. Debris clearance for road access

RESOURCE MOBILIZATION

Red Cross/Red Crescent has deployed 19 emergency response units. WFP pre-positioned stocks being distributed. Government allocated emergency fund of $443M for immediate relief operations.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for earthquake, severity: critical, location: Colombia, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

A magnitude 8.1 earthquake struck Bogota, Colombia at 18:12 local time, with the epicentre located 44km underground. The quake caused widespread structural damage across the metropolitan area. 523 deaths confirmed, 24,018 injured, and an estimated 457,531 people displaced from their homes.

DAMAGE ASSESSMENT

- 29,003 buildings collapsed or severely damaged
- Critical infrastructure: bridges damaged
- Hospitals operating at reduced capacity
- Power outages affecting 253,787 households
- Road access disrupted in 35 areas

HUMANITARIAN NEEDS

Immediate priorities include urban search and rescue, emergency medical care, temporary shelter, and restoration of water supply. Aftershocks continuing, complicating rescue efforts.

COORDINATION

UN OCHA has activated the cluster system. UNDAC team deployed. International search and rescue teams from 4 countries arriving. Government has requested international assistance.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for drought, severity: critical, location: Mozambique, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Prolonged drought conditions in Mozambique have entered their 6th consecutive month, with rainfall at 22% of seasonal average. An estimated 958,415 people are facing acute food insecurity across 12 regions.

FOOD SECURITY

- IPC Phase 3 (Crisis): 637,904 people
- IPC Phase 4 (Emergency): 499,129 people
- Livestock losses: 257,638 animals
- Crop production: 31% below average

WATER SITUATION

41% of water sources dried up or critically low. Water trucking operations serving 164,152 people daily but demand far exceeds current capacity.

HUMANITARIAN RESPONSE

FAO and WFP scaling up emergency food assistance. Supplementary feeding programmes targeting 112,875 malnourished children under 5. Cash transfer programmes reaching 37,301 households.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for cyclone, severity: high, location: Japan, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Tropical Cyclone Idai made landfall near Tokyo, Japan as a Category 2 storm with sustained winds of 143km/h. Storm surge of 3.6m reported along the coastline. 788 fatalities confirmed, with 354 persons still missing.

IMPACT

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for flood, severity: critical, location: India, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Heavy monsoon rains have caused severe flooding in Mumbai, India, displacing approximately 461,748 people across 5 districts. Water levels have risen to 6.1m above normal, submerging residential areas and critical infrastructure. 998 fatalities have been confirmed so far.

HUMANITARIAN NEEDS

- Emergency shelter for 461,748 displaced persons
- Clean drinking water — municipal supply contaminated
- Medical supplies: waterborne disease prevention kits
- Food supplies for an estimated 174,943 affected individuals
- Search and rescue operations in 24 submerged neighborhoods

RESPONSE ACTIONS

National disaster management authority has declared a state of emergency. Military helicopters deployed for evacuation. International aid agencies mobilising emergency response teams. Temporary shelters established at 57 locations including schools and community centers.

RESOURCE REQUIREMENTS

- Water purification units: 51
- Emergency tents: 12,508
- Medical teams: 32
- Rescue boats: 24
- Food ration packs: 74,246

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for earthquake, severity: critical, location: Ethiopia, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

A magnitude 7.1 earthquake struck Addis Ababa, Ethiopia at 04:26 local time, with the epicentre located 78km underground. The quake caused widespread structural damage across the metropolitan area. 50 deaths confirmed, 37,464 injured, and an estimated 458,172 people displaced from their homes.

DAMAGE ASSESSMENT

- 3,276 buildings collapsed or severely damaged
- Critical infrastructure: airport runway cracked
- Hospitals operating at reduced capacity
- Power outages affecting 382,818 households
- Road access disrupted in 7 areas

HUMANITARIAN NEEDS

Immediate priorities include urban search and rescue, emergency medical care, temporary shelter, and restoration of water supply. Aftershocks continuing, complicating rescue efforts.

COORDINATION

UN OCHA has activated the cluster system. UNDAC team deployed. International search and rescue teams from 5 countries arriving. Government has requested international assistance.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for drought, severity: critical, location: Mozambique, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Prolonged drought conditions in Mozambique have entered their 5th consecutive month, with rainfall at 23% of seasonal average. An estimated 750,516 people are facing acute food insecurity across 3 regions.

FOOD SECURITY

- IPC Phase 3 (Crisis): 1,974,836 people
- IPC Phase 4 (Emergency): 459,634 people
- Livestock losses: 382,335 animals
- Crop production: 65% below average

WATER SITUATION

54% of water sources dried up or critically low. Water trucking operations serving 37,876 people daily but demand far exceeds current capacity.

HUMANITARIAN RESPONSE

FAO and WFP scaling up emergency food assistance. Supplementary feeding programmes targeting 89,368 malnourished children under 5. Cash transfer programmes reaching 52,592 households.

Tropical Cyclone Irma made landfall near Sanaa, Yemen as a Category 2 storm with sustained winds of 139km/h. Storm surge of 7.9m reported along the coastline. 1290 fatalities confirmed, with 446 persons still missing.

- 271,486 people evacuated from coastal communities
- Extensive damage to crops affecting 63,489 hectares
- Fishing fleet: 786 vessels damaged or destroyed
- Communication networks disrupted across 13 provinces

RESPONSE PRIORITIES

5. Debris clearance for road access

RESOURCE MOBILIZATION

Red Cross/Red Crescent has deployed 16 emergency response units. WFP pre-positioned stocks being distributed. Government allocated emergency fund of $333M for immediate relief operations.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for epidemic, severity: high, location: Nepal, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

A malaria outbreak has been declared in Nepal, with 26,335 confirmed cases and 569 deaths reported across 18 provinces as of today. Case fatality rate stands at 8.5%. The outbreak originated in Kathmandu and has spread to neighbouring areas.

HEALTH RESPONSE

- 40 treatment centers established
- 1,963 health workers deployed
- Contact tracing: 32,928 contacts identified and monitored
- Vaccination campaign targeting 1,168,748 people

CHALLENGES

Limited laboratory capacity, inadequate medical supplies, community resistance to health measures, and cross-border movement complicating containment efforts.

RESOURCE NEEDS

WHO has classified this as a Grade 3 emergency. Urgent need for medical supplies, PPE, diagnostic kits, and additional health personnel. Estimated funding gap: $107M.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for earthquake, severity: critical, location: Indonesia, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

A magnitude 6.1 earthquake struck Jakarta, Indonesia at 07:28 local time, with the epicentre located 36km underground. The quake caused widespread structural damage across the metropolitan area. 867 deaths confirmed, 31,678 injured, and an estimated 121,221 people displaced from their homes.

DAMAGE ASSESSMENT

- 1,030 buildings collapsed or severely damaged
- Critical infrastructure: water main ruptured
- Hospitals operating at reduced capacity
- Power outages affecting 27,355 households
- Road access disrupted in 40 areas

HUMANITARIAN NEEDS

Immediate priorities include urban search and rescue, emergency medical care, temporary shelter, and restoration of water supply. Aftershocks continuing, complicating rescue efforts.

COORDINATION

UN OCHA has activated the cluster system. UNDAC team deployed. International search and rescue teams from 7 countries arriving. Government has requested international assistance.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for flood, severity: critical, location: Somalia, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Heavy monsoon rains have caused severe flooding in Mogadishu, Somalia, displacing approximately 267,213 people across 9 districts. Water levels have risen to 7.4m above normal, submerging residential areas and critical infrastructure. 998 fatalities have been confirmed so far.

HUMANITARIAN NEEDS

- Emergency shelter for 267,213 displaced persons
- Clean drinking water — municipal supply contaminated
- Medical supplies: waterborne disease prevention kits
- Food supplies for an estimated 197,564 affected individuals
- Search and rescue operations in 11 submerged neighborhoods

RESPONSE ACTIONS

National disaster management authority has declared a state of emergency. Military helicopters deployed for evacuation. International aid agencies mobilising emergency response teams. Temporary shelters established at 38 locations including schools and community centers.

RESOURCE REQUIREMENTS

- Water purification units: 194
- Emergency tents: 8,409
- Medical teams: 40
- Rescue boats: 126
- Food ration packs: 46,091

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for drought, severity: critical, location: Afghanistan, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Prolonged drought conditions in Afghanistan have entered their 18th consecutive month, with rainfall at 15% of seasonal average. An estimated 3,720,522 people are facing acute food insecurity across 12 regions.

FOOD SECURITY

- IPC Phase 3 (Crisis): 71,622 people
- IPC Phase 4 (Emergency): 209,291 people
- Livestock losses: 125,004 animals
- Crop production: 58% below average

WATER SITUATION

81% of water sources dried up or critically low. Water trucking operations serving 179,932 people daily but demand far exceeds current capacity.

HUMANITARIAN RESPONSE

FAO and WFP scaling up emergency food assistance. Supplementary feeding programmes targeting 20,068 malnourished children under 5. Cash transfer programmes reaching 94,609 households.

- 455,287 people evacuated from coastal communities
- Extensive damage to crops affecting 71,181 hectares
- Fishing fleet: 2000 vessels damaged or destroyed
- Communication networks disrupted across 6 provinces

RESPONSE PRIORITIES

1. Search and rescue in affected coastal communities
2. Emergency shelter and food distribution
3. Restoration of clean water supply
4. Medical response — injury treatment and disease prevention
5. Debris clearance for road access

RESOURCE MOBILIZATION

Red Cross/Red Crescent has deployed 13 emergency response units. WFP pre-positioned stocks being distributed. Government allocated emergency fund of $302M for immediate relief operations.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for epidemic, severity: high, location: Myanmar, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

A dengue fever outbreak has been declared in Myanmar, with 44,083 confirmed cases and 322 deaths reported across 14 provinces as of today. Case fatality rate stands at 2.0%. The outbreak originated in Yangon and has spread to neighbouring areas.

HEALTH RESPONSE

- 27 treatment centers established
- 3,119 health workers deployed
- Contact tracing: 20,605 contacts identified and monitored
- Vaccination campaign targeting 1,644,142 people

CHALLENGES

Limited laboratory capacity, inadequate medical supplies, community resistance to health measures, and cross-border movement complicating containment efforts.

RESOURCE NEEDS

WHO has classified this as a Grade 2 emergency. Urgent need for medical supplies, PPE, diagnostic kits, and additional health personnel. Estimated funding gap: $199M.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for earthquake, severity: critical, location: Mozambique, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

A magnitude 5.7 earthquake struck Maputo, Mozambique at 03:22 local time, with the epicentre located 58km underground. The quake caused widespread structural damage across the metropolitan area. 1015 deaths confirmed, 30,601 injured, and an estimated 285,151 people displaced from their homes.

DAMAGE ASSESSMENT

- 17,894 buildings collapsed or severely damaged
- Critical infrastructure: water main ruptured
- Hospitals operating at reduced capacity
- Power outages affecting 309,405 households
- Road access disrupted in 6 areas

HUMANITARIAN NEEDS

Immediate priorities include urban search and rescue, emergency medical care, temporary shelter, and restoration of water supply. Aftershocks continuing, complicating rescue efforts.

COORDINATION

UN OCHA has activated the cluster system. UNDAC team deployed. International search and rescue teams from 9 countries arriving. Government has requested international assistance.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for drought, severity: critical, location: Yemen, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Prolonged drought conditions in Yemen have entered their 6th consecutive month, with rainfall at 27% of seasonal average. An estimated 4,944,065 people are facing acute food insecurity across 3 regions.

FOOD SECURITY

- IPC Phase 3 (Crisis): 201,099 people
- IPC Phase 4 (Emergency): 345,350 people
- Livestock losses: 419,685 animals
- Crop production: 57% below average

WATER SITUATION

45% of water sources dried up or critically low. Water trucking operations serving 67,882 people daily but demand far exceeds current capacity.

HUMANITARIAN RESPONSE

FAO and WFP scaling up emergency food assistance. Supplementary feeding programmes targeting 147,491 malnourished children under 5. Cash transfer programmes reaching 78,478 households.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for flood, severity: critical, location: Mozambique, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Heavy monsoon rains have caused severe flooding in Maputo, Mozambique, displacing approximately 234,026 people across 6 districts. Water levels have risen to 1.7m above normal, submerging residential areas and critical infrastructure. 792 fatalities have been confirmed so far.

HUMANITARIAN NEEDS

- Emergency shelter for 234,026 displaced persons
- Clean drinking water — municipal supply contaminated
- Medical supplies: waterborne disease prevention kits
- Food supplies for an estimated 44,298 affected individuals
- Search and rescue operations in 24 submerged neighborhoods

RESPONSE ACTIONS

National disaster management authority has declared a state of emergency. Military helicopters deployed for evacuation. International aid agencies mobilising emergency response teams. Temporary shelters established at 25 locations including schools and community centers.

RESOURCE REQUIREMENTS

- Water purification units: 150
- Emergency tents: 17,904
- Medical teams: 29
- Rescue boats: 117
- Food ration packs: 420,890

SITUATION OVERVIEW

A magnitude 5.9 earthquake struck Karachi, Pakistan at 23:40 local time, with the epicentre located 76km underground. The quake caused widespread structural damage across the metropolitan area. 1832 deaths confirmed, 31,129 injured, and an estimated 321,885 people displaced from their homes.

DAMAGE ASSESSMENT

- 17,781 buildings collapsed or severely damaged
- Critical infrastructure: major hospital partially collapsed
- Hospitals operating at reduced capacity
- Power outages affecting 177,241 households
- Road access disrupted in 38 areas

HUMANITARIAN NEEDS

Immediate priorities include urban search and rescue, emergency medical care, temporary shelter, and restoration of water supply. Aftershocks continuing, complicating rescue efforts.

COORDINATION

UN OCHA has activated the cluster system. UNDAC team deployed. International search and rescue teams from 14 countries arriving. Government has requested international assistance.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for flood, severity: critical, location: Nepal, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Heavy monsoon rains have caused severe flooding in Kathmandu, Nepal, displacing approximately 447,226 people across 23 districts. Water levels have risen to 3.8m above normal, submerging residential areas and critical infrastructure. 316 fatalities have been confirmed so far.

HUMANITARIAN NEEDS

- Emergency shelter for 447,226 displaced persons
- Clean drinking water — municipal supply contaminated
- Medical supplies: waterborne disease prevention kits
- Food supplies for an estimated 84,868 affected individuals
- Search and rescue operations in 9 submerged neighborhoods

RESPONSE ACTIONS

National disaster management authority has declared a state of emergency. Military helicopters deployed for evacuation. International aid agencies mobilising emergency response teams. Temporary shelters established at 79 locations including schools and community centers.

RESOURCE REQUIREMENTS

- Water purification units: 33
- Emergency tents: 12,170
- Medical teams: 25
- Rescue boats: 49
- Food ration packs: 425,137

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for drought, severity: critical, location: Pakistan, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Prolonged drought conditions in Pakistan have entered their 12th consecutive month, with rainfall at 40% of seasonal average. An estimated 2,213,211 people are facing acute food insecurity across 11 regions.

FOOD SECURITY

- IPC Phase 3 (Crisis): 1,779,629 people
- IPC Phase 4 (Emergency): 186,546 people
- Livestock losses: 179,571 animals
- Crop production: 61% below average

WATER SITUATION

53% of water sources dried up or critically low. Water trucking operations serving 68,231 people daily but demand far exceeds current capacity.

HUMANITARIAN RESPONSE

FAO and WFP scaling up emergency food assistance. Supplementary feeding programmes targeting 193,000 malnourished children under 5. Cash transfer programmes reaching 17,553 households.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for cyclone, severity: high, location: Mozambique, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Tropical Cyclone Dorian made landfall near Maputo, Mozambique as a Category 3 storm with sustained winds of 270km/h. Storm surge of 3.8m reported along the coastline. 1883 fatalities confirmed, with 426 persons still missing.

IMPACT

- 186,639 people evacuated from coastal communities
- Extensive damage to crops affecting 115,055 hectares
- Fishing fleet: 993 vessels damaged or destroyed
- Communication networks disrupted across 5 provinces

RESPONSE PRIORITIES

1. Search and rescue in affected coastal communities
2. Emergency shelter and food distribution
3. Restoration of clean water supply
4. Medical response — injury treatment and disease prevention
5. Debris clearance for road access

RESOURCE MOBILIZATION

Red Cross/Red Crescent has deployed 8 emergency response units. WFP pre-positioned stocks being distributed. Government allocated emergency fund of $333M for immediate relief operations.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for epidemic, severity: high, location: Somalia, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

A Ebola outbreak has been declared in Somalia, with 38,188 confirmed cases and 1405 deaths reported across 16 provinces as of today. Case fatality rate stands at 20.6%. The outbreak originated in Mogadishu and has spread to neighbouring areas.

HEALTH RESPONSE

- 20 treatment centers established
- 4,682 health workers deployed
- Contact tracing: 18,754 contacts identified and monitored
- Vaccination campaign targeting 1,469,525 people

CHALLENGES

Limited laboratory capacity, inadequate medical supplies, community resistance to health measures, and cross-border movement complicating containment efforts.

RESOURCE NEEDS

WHO has classified this as a Grade 2 emergency. Urgent need for medical supplies, PPE, diagnostic kits, and additional health personnel. Estimated funding gap: $195M.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for drought, severity: critical, location: Haiti, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Prolonged drought conditions in Haiti have entered their 6th consecutive month, with rainfall at 28% of seasonal average. An estimated 1,231,937 people are facing acute food insecurity across 4 regions.

FOOD SECURITY

- IPC Phase 3 (Crisis): 1,231,762 people
- IPC Phase 4 (Emergency): 269,446 people
- Livestock losses: 91,829 animals
- Crop production: 33% below average

WATER SITUATION

82% of water sources dried up or critically low. Water trucking operations serving 46,632 people daily but demand far exceeds current capacity.

HUMANITARIAN RESPONSE

FAO and WFP scaling up emergency food assistance. Supplementary feeding programmes targeting 56,442 malnourished children under 5. Cash transfer programmes reaching 85,934 households.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for earthquake, severity: critical, location: Indonesia, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

A magnitude 8.1 earthquake struck Jakarta, Indonesia at 10:49 local time, with the epicentre located 32km underground. The quake caused widespread structural damage across the metropolitan area. 806 deaths confirmed, 15,106 injured, and an estimated 365,911 people displaced from their homes.

DAMAGE ASSESSMENT

- 28,868 buildings collapsed or severely damaged
- Critical infrastructure: major hospital partially collapsed
- Hospitals operating at reduced capacity
- Power outages affecting 392,421 households
- Road access disrupted in 25 areas

HUMANITARIAN NEEDS

Immediate priorities include urban search and rescue, emergency medical care, temporary shelter, and restoration of water supply. Aftershocks continuing, complicating rescue efforts.

COORDINATION

UN OCHA has activated the cluster system. UNDAC team deployed. International search and rescue teams from 4 countries arriving. Government has requested international assistance.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for drought, severity: critical, location: Turkey, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Prolonged drought conditions in Turkey have entered their 16th consecutive month, with rainfall at 40% of seasonal average. An estimated 1,300,054 people are facing acute food insecurity across 11 regions.

FOOD SECURITY

- IPC Phase 3 (Crisis): 293,907 people
- IPC Phase 4 (Emergency): 40,022 people
- Livestock losses: 176,753 animals
- Crop production: 37% below average

WATER SITUATION

78% of water sources dried up or critically low. Water trucking operations serving 105,356 people daily but demand far exceeds current capacity.

HUMANITARIAN RESPONSE

FAO and WFP scaling up emergency food assistance. Supplementary feeding programmes targeting 97,304 malnourished children under 5. Cash transfer programmes reaching 44,060 households.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for flood, severity: critical, location: Syria, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Heavy monsoon rains have caused severe flooding in Aleppo, Syria, displacing approximately 421,901 people across 25 districts. Water levels have risen to 7.1m above normal, submerging residential areas and critical infrastructure. 1229 fatalities have been confirmed so far.

HUMANITARIAN NEEDS

- Emergency shelter for 421,901 displaced persons
- Clean drinking water — municipal supply contaminated
- Medical supplies: waterborne disease prevention kits
- Food supplies for an estimated 118,372 affected individuals
- Search and rescue operations in 26 submerged neighborhoods

RESPONSE ACTIONS

National disaster management authority has declared a state of emergency. Military helicopters deployed for evacuation. International aid agencies mobilising emergency response teams. Temporary shelters established at 99 locations including schools and community centers.

RESOURCE REQUIREMENTS

- Water purification units: 107
- Emergency tents: 5,954
- Medical teams: 18
- Rescue boats: 76
- Food ration packs: 357,351

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for epidemic, severity: high, location: Ethiopia, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

A dengue fever outbreak has been declared in Ethiopia, with 11,068 confirmed cases and 1668 deaths reported across 13 provinces as of today. Case fatality rate stands at 24.5%. The outbreak originated in Addis Ababa and has spread to neighbouring areas.

HEALTH RESPONSE

- 15 treatment centers established
- 1,795 health workers deployed
- Contact tracing: 6,473 contacts identified and monitored
- Vaccination campaign targeting 700,353 people

CHALLENGES

Limited laboratory capacity, inadequate medical supplies, community resistance to health measures, and cross-border movement complicating containment efforts.

RESOURCE NEEDS

WHO has classified this as a Grade 1 emergency. Urgent need for medical supplies, PPE, diagnostic kits, and additional health personnel. Estimated funding gap: $182M.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for earthquake, severity: critical, location: Nepal, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

A magnitude 8.0 earthquake struck Kathmandu, Nepal at 18:07 local time, with the epicentre located 67km underground. The quake caused widespread structural damage across the metropolitan area. 493 deaths confirmed, 35,511 injured, and an estimated 396,572 people displaced from their homes.

DAMAGE ASSESSMENT

- 4,515 buildings collapsed or severely damaged
- Critical infrastructure: bridges damaged
- Hospitals operating at reduced capacity
- Power outages affecting 249,981 households
- Road access disrupted in 26 areas

HUMANITARIAN NEEDS

Immediate priorities include urban search and rescue, emergency medical care, temporary shelter, and restoration of water supply. Aftershocks continuing, complicating rescue efforts.

COORDINATION

UN OCHA has activated the cluster system. UNDAC team deployed. International search and rescue teams from 11 countries arriving. Government has requested international assistance.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for flood, severity: critical, location: Somalia, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:

Heavy monsoon rains have caused severe flooding in Mogadishu, Somalia, displacing approximately 107,953 people across 17 districts. Water levels have risen to 1.6m above normal, submerging residential areas and critical infrastructure. 1792 fatalities have been confirmed so far.

HUMANITARIAN NEEDS

- Emergency tents: 997
- Medical teams: 23
- Rescue boats: 107
- Food ration packs: 196,899

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for earthquake, severity: critical, location: Somalia, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:

A magnitude 8.3 earthquake struck Mogadishu, Somalia at 02:56 local time, with the epicentre located 44km underground. The quake caused widespread structural damage across the metropolitan area. 1724 deaths confirmed, 11,587 injured, and an estimated 222,445 people displaced from their homes.

DAMAGE ASSESSMENT

- 27,226 buildings collapsed or severely damaged
- Critical infrastructure: bridges damaged
- Hospitals operating at reduced capacity
- Power outages affecting 328,635 households
- Road access disrupted in 35 areas

HUMANITARIAN NEEDS

COORDINATION

UN OCHA has activated the cluster system. UNDAC team deployed. International search and rescue teams from 10 countries arriving. Government has requested international assistance.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for cyclone, severity: high, location: Bangladesh, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Tropical Cyclone Dorian made landfall near Dhaka, Bangladesh as a Category 4 storm with sustained winds of 139km/h. Storm surge of 3.0m reported along the coastline. 1672 fatalities confirmed, with 437 persons still missing.

IMPACT

- 402,646 people evacuated from coastal communities
- Extensive damage to crops affecting 121,481 hectares
- Fishing fleet: 467 vessels damaged or destroyed
- Communication networks disrupted across 12 provinces

RESPONSE PRIORITIES

1. Search and rescue in affected coastal communities
2. Emergency shelter and food distribution
3. Restoration of clean water supply
4. Medical response — injury treatment and disease prevention
5. Debris clearance for road access

RESOURCE MOBILIZATION

Red Cross/Red Crescent has deployed 30 emergency response units. WFP pre-positioned stocks being distributed. Government allocated emergency fund of $331M for immediate relief operations.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for flood, severity: critical, location: Indonesia, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Heavy monsoon rains have caused severe flooding in Jakarta, Indonesia, displacing approximately 86,660 people across 3 districts. Water levels have risen to 4.2m above normal, submerging residential areas and critical infrastructure. 1060 fatalities have been confirmed so far.

HUMANITARIAN NEEDS

- Emergency shelter for 86,660 displaced persons
- Clean drinking water — municipal supply contaminated
- Medical supplies: waterborne disease prevention kits
- Food supplies for an estimated 44,708 affected individuals
- Search and rescue operations in 14 submerged neighborhoods

RESPONSE ACTIONS

National disaster management authority has declared a state of emergency. Military helicopters deployed for evacuation. International aid agencies mobilising emergency response teams. Temporary shelters established at 17 locations including schools and community centers.

RESOURCE REQUIREMENTS

- Water purification units: 39
- Emergency tents: 2,040
- Medical teams: 21
- Rescue boats: 34
- Food ration packs: 345,651

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for drought, severity: critical, location: Nigeria, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Prolonged drought conditions in Nigeria have entered their 6th consecutive month, with rainfall at 12% of seasonal average. An estimated 3,232,422 people are facing acute food insecurity across 8 regions.

FOOD SECURITY

- IPC Phase 3 (Crisis): 1,199,241 people
- IPC Phase 4 (Emergency): 228,409 people
- Livestock losses: 65,965 animals
- Crop production: 64% below average

WATER SITUATION

47% of water sources dried up or critically low. Water trucking operations serving 160,720 people daily but demand far exceeds current capacity.

HUMANITARIAN RESPONSE

FAO and WFP scaling up emergency food assistance. Supplementary feeding programmes targeting 114,060 malnourished children under 5. Cash transfer programmes reaching 11,090 households.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for earthquake, severity: critical, location: Turkey, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

A magnitude 6.7 earthquake struck Istanbul, Turkey at 20:33 local time, with the epicentre located 27km underground. The quake caused widespread structural damage across the metropolitan area. 1128 deaths confirmed, 42,500 injured, and an estimated 218,421 people displaced from their homes.

DAMAGE ASSESSMENT

- 21,071 buildings collapsed or severely damaged
- Critical infrastructure: water main ruptured
- Hospitals operating at reduced capacity
- Power outages affecting 303,891 households
- Road access disrupted in 20 areas

HUMANITARIAN NEEDS

Immediate priorities include urban search and rescue, emergency medical care, temporary shelter, and restoration of water supply. Aftershocks continuing, complicating rescue efforts.

COORDINATION

UN OCHA has activated the cluster system. UNDAC team deployed. International search and rescue teams from 12 countries arriving. Government has requested international assistance.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for drought, severity: critical, location: Haiti, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Prolonged drought conditions in Haiti have entered their 10th consecutive month, with rainfall at 15% of seasonal average. An estimated 2,797,785 people are facing acute food insecurity across 12 regions.

FOOD SECURITY

- IPC Phase 3 (Crisis): 1,092,979 people
- IPC Phase 4 (Emergency): 495,287 people
- Livestock losses: 33,782 animals
- Crop production: 37% below average

WATER SITUATION

84% of water sources dried up or critically low. Water trucking operations serving 40,315 people daily but demand far exceeds current capacity.

HUMANITARIAN RESPONSE

FAO and WFP scaling up emergency food assistance. Supplementary feeding programmes targeting 104,948 malnourished children under 5. Cash transfer programmes reaching 45,055 households.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for cyclone, severity: high, location: Kenya, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Tropical Cyclone Irma made landfall near Nairobi, Kenya as a Category 3 storm with sustained winds of 212km/h. Storm surge of 5.9m reported along the coastline. 804 fatalities confirmed, with 100 persons still missing.

IMPACT

- 153,894 people evacuated from coastal communities
- Extensive damage to crops affecting 92,996 hectares
- Fishing fleet: 841 vessels damaged or destroyed
- Communication networks disrupted across 6 provinces

RESPONSE PRIORITIES

1. Search and rescue in affected coastal communities
2. Emergency shelter and food distribution
3. Restoration of clean water supply
4. Medical response — injury treatment and disease prevention
5. Debris clearance for road access

RESOURCE MOBILIZATION

Red Cross/Red Crescent has deployed 18 emergency response units. WFP pre-positioned stocks being distributed. Government allocated emergency fund of $161M for immediate relief operations.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for earthquake, severity: critical, location: Yemen, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

A magnitude 6.1 earthquake struck Sanaa, Yemen at 21:34 local time, with the epicentre located 46km underground. The quake caused widespread structural damage across the metropolitan area. 185 deaths confirmed, 4,477 injured, and an estimated 41,321 people displaced from their homes.

DAMAGE ASSESSMENT

- 2,958 buildings collapsed or severely damaged
- Critical infrastructure: airport runway cracked
- Hospitals operating at reduced capacity
- Power outages affecting 19,630 households
- Road access disrupted in 21 areas

HUMANITARIAN NEEDS

Immediate priorities include urban search and rescue, emergency medical care, temporary shelter, and restoration of water supply. Aftershocks continuing, complicating rescue efforts.

COORDINATION

UN OCHA has activated the cluster system. UNDAC team deployed. International search and rescue teams from 15 countries arriving. Government has requested international assistance.

DAMAGE ASSESSMENT

UN OCHA has activated the cluster system. UNDAC team deployed. International search and rescue teams from 8 countries arriving. Government has requested international assistance.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for epidemic, severity: high, location: Japan, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

A dengue fever outbreak has been declared in Japan, with 35,620 confirmed cases and 315 deaths reported across 5 provinces as of today. Case fatality rate stands at 12.1%. The outbreak originated in Tokyo and has spread to neighbouring areas.

HEALTH RESPONSE

- 16 treatment centers established
- 4,919 health workers deployed
- Contact tracing: 39,004 contacts identified and monitored
- Vaccination campaign targeting 811,342 people

CHALLENGES

Limited laboratory capacity, inadequate medical supplies, community resistance to health measures, and cross-border movement complicating containment efforts.

RESOURCE NEEDS

WHO has classified this as a Grade 1 emergency. Urgent need for medical supplies, PPE, diagnostic kits, and additional health personnel. Estimated funding gap: $40M.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for cyclone, severity: high, location: Turkey, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Tropical Cyclone Haiyan made landfall near Istanbul, Turkey as a Category 4 storm with sustained winds of 156km/h. Storm surge of 5.3m reported along the coastline. 1795 fatalities confirmed, with 447 persons still missing.

IMPACT

- 348,216 people evacuated from coastal communities
- Extensive damage to crops affecting 126,389 hectares
- Fishing fleet: 84 vessels damaged or destroyed
- Communication networks disrupted across 10 provinces

RESPONSE PRIORITIES

1. Search and rescue in affected coastal communities
2. Emergency shelter and food distribution
3. Restoration of clean water supply
4. Medical response — injury treatment and disease prevention
5. Debris clearance for road access

RESOURCE MOBILIZATION

Red Cross/Red Crescent has deployed 15 emergency response units. WFP pre-positioned stocks being distributed. Government allocated emergency fund of $199M for immediate relief operations.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for flood, severity: critical, location: Colombia, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Heavy monsoon rains have caused severe flooding in Bogota, Colombia, displacing approximately 104,983 people across 17 districts. Water levels have risen to 5.2m above normal, submerging residential areas and critical infrastructure. 1780 fatalities have been confirmed so far.

HUMANITARIAN NEEDS

- Emergency shelter for 104,983 displaced persons
- Clean drinking water — municipal supply contaminated
- Medical supplies: waterborne disease prevention kits
- Food supplies for an estimated 112,906 affected individuals
- Search and rescue operations in 15 submerged neighborhoods

RESPONSE ACTIONS

National disaster management authority has declared a state of emergency. Military helicopters deployed for evacuation. International aid agencies mobilising emergency response teams. Temporary shelters established at 67 locations including schools and community centers.

RESOURCE REQUIREMENTS

- Water purification units: 31
- Emergency tents: 12,674
- Medical teams: 20
- Rescue boats: 93
- Food ration packs: 122,498

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for drought, severity: critical, location: Japan, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Prolonged drought conditions in Japan have entered their 11th consecutive month, with rainfall at 14% of seasonal average. An estimated 3,854,790 people are facing acute food insecurity across 6 regions.

FOOD SECURITY

- IPC Phase 3 (Crisis): 221,623 people
- IPC Phase 4 (Emergency): 295,690 people
- Livestock losses: 217,647 animals
- Crop production: 66% below average

WATER SITUATION

66% of water sources dried up or critically low. Water trucking operations serving 136,215 people daily but demand far exceeds current capacity.

HUMANITARIAN RESPONSE

FAO and WFP scaling up emergency food assistance. Supplementary feeding programmes targeting 91,786 malnourished children under 5. Cash transfer programmes reaching 37,794 households.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for epidemic, severity: high, location: Mozambique, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

A typhoid outbreak has been declared in Mozambique, with 12,903 confirmed cases and 1176 deaths reported across 12 provinces as of today. Case fatality rate stands at 1.7%. The outbreak originated in Maputo and has spread to neighbouring areas.

HEALTH RESPONSE

- 32 treatment centers established
- 1,191 health workers deployed
- Contact tracing: 36,721 contacts identified and monitored
- Vaccination campaign targeting 1,419,166 people

CHALLENGES

Limited laboratory capacity, inadequate medical supplies, community resistance to health measures, and cross-border movement complicating containment efforts.

RESOURCE NEEDS

WHO has classified this as a Grade 3 emergency. Urgent need for medical supplies, PPE, diagnostic kits, and additional health personnel. Estimated funding gap: $152M.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for cyclone, severity: high, location: South Sudan, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Tropical Cyclone Nargis made landfall near Juba, South Sudan as a Category 2 storm with sustained winds of 141km/h. Storm surge of 2.5m reported along the coastline. 881 fatalities confirmed, with 359 persons still missing.

IMPACT

- 145,384 people evacuated from coastal communities
- Extensive damage to crops affecting 83,265 hectares
- Fishing fleet: 1375 vessels damaged or destroyed
- Communication networks disrupted across 6 provinces

RESPONSE PRIORITIES

1. Search and rescue in affected coastal communities
2. Emergency shelter and food distribution
3. Restoration of clean water supply
4. Medical response — injury treatment and disease prevention
5. Debris clearance for road access

RESOURCE MOBILIZATION

Red Cross/Red Crescent has deployed 14 emergency response units. WFP pre-positioned stocks being distributed. Government allocated emergency fund of $404M for immediate relief operations.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for drought, severity: critical, location: Nigeria, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Prolonged drought conditions in Nigeria have entered their 16th consecutive month, with rainfall at 20% of seasonal average. An estimated 4,805,328 people are facing acute food insecurity across 11 regions.

FOOD SECURITY

- IPC Phase 3 (Crisis): 1,923,231 people
- IPC Phase 4 (Emergency): 310,181 people
- Livestock losses: 31,369 animals
- Crop production: 59% below average

WATER SITUATION

72% of water sources dried up or critically low. Water trucking operations serving 59,428 people daily but demand far exceeds current capacity.

HUMANITARIAN RESPONSE

FAO and WFP scaling up emergency food assistance. Supplementary feeding programmes targeting 115,126 malnourished children under 5. Cash transfer programmes reaching 22,179 households.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for epidemic, severity: high, location: Japan, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

A malaria outbreak has been declared in Japan, with 16,834 confirmed cases and 1203 deaths reported across 7 provinces as of today. Case fatality rate stands at 18.7%. The outbreak originated in Tokyo and has spread to neighbouring areas.

HEALTH RESPONSE

- 6 treatment centers established
- 1,473 health workers deployed
- Contact tracing: 28,995 contacts identified and monitored
- Vaccination campaign targeting 441,789 people

CHALLENGES

Limited laboratory capacity, inadequate medical supplies, community resistance to health measures, and cross-border movement complicating containment efforts.

RESOURCE NEEDS

WHO has classified this as a Grade 1 emergency. Urgent need for medical supplies, PPE, diagnostic kits, and additional health personnel. Estimated funding gap: $159M.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for earthquake, severity: critical, location: Yemen, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

A magnitude 8.0 earthquake struck Sanaa, Yemen at 02:48 local time, with the epicentre located 61km underground. The quake caused widespread structural damage across the metropolitan area. 1956 deaths confirmed, 10,737 injured, and an estimated 274,106 people displaced from their homes.

DAMAGE ASSESSMENT

- 12,016 buildings collapsed or severely damaged
- Critical infrastructure: major hospital partially collapsed
- Hospitals operating at reduced capacity
- Power outages affecting 436,927 households
- Road access disrupted in 33 areas

HUMANITARIAN NEEDS

Immediate priorities include urban search and rescue, emergency medical care, temporary shelter, and restoration of water supply. Aftershocks continuing, complicating rescue efforts.

COORDINATION

UN OCHA has activated the cluster system. UNDAC team deployed. International search and rescue teams from 5 countries arriving. Government has requested international assistance.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for cyclone, severity: high, location: Mozambique, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Tropical Cyclone Dorian made landfall near Maputo, Mozambique as a Category 4 storm with sustained winds of 142km/h. Storm surge of 6.9m reported along the coastline. 1916 fatalities confirmed, with 233 persons still missing.

IMPACT

- 464,174 people evacuated from coastal communities
- Extensive damage to crops affecting 171,580 hectares
- Fishing fleet: 1422 vessels damaged or destroyed
- Communication networks disrupted across 4 provinces

RESPONSE PRIORITIES

1. Search and rescue in affected coastal communities
2. Emergency shelter and food distribution
3. Restoration of clean water supply
4. Medical response — injury treatment and disease prevention
5. Debris clearance for road access

RESOURCE MOBILIZATION

Red Cross/Red Crescent has deployed 7 emergency response units. WFP pre-positioned stocks being distributed. Government allocated emergency fund of $493M for immediate relief operations.

A magnitude 6.1 earthquake struck Manila, Philippines at 15:14 local time, with the epicentre located 44km underground. The quake caused widespread structural damage across the metropolitan area. 1573 deaths confirmed, 49,195 injured, and an estimated 319,329 people displaced from their homes.

DAMAGE ASSESSMENT

- Critical infrastructure: airport runway cracked
- Hospitals operating at reduced capacity
- Power outages affecting 311,060 households

COORDINATION

UN OCHA has activated the cluster system. UNDAC team deployed. International search and rescue teams from 3 countries arriving. Government has requested international assistance.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for cyclone, severity: high, location: Indonesia, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Tropical Cyclone Dorian made landfall near Jakarta, Indonesia as a Category 4 storm with sustained winds of 282km/h. Storm surge of 3.3m reported along the coastline. 1221 fatalities confirmed, with 87 persons still missing.

IMPACT

- 146,430 people evacuated from coastal communities
- Extensive damage to crops affecting 185,717 hectares
- Fishing fleet: 754 vessels damaged or destroyed
- Communication networks disrupted across 11 provinces

RESPONSE PRIORITIES

1. Search and rescue in affected coastal communities
2. Emergency shelter and food distribution
3. Restoration of clean water supply
4. Medical response — injury treatment and disease prevention
5. Debris clearance for road access

RESOURCE MOBILIZATION

Red Cross/Red Crescent has deployed 15 emergency response units. WFP pre-positioned stocks being distributed. Government allocated emergency fund of $43M for immediate relief operations.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for drought, severity: critical, location: Japan, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Prolonged drought conditions in Japan have entered their 14th consecutive month, with rainfall at 29% of seasonal average. An estimated 566,884 people are facing acute food insecurity across 5 regions.

FOOD SECURITY

- IPC Phase 3 (Crisis): 72,228 people
- IPC Phase 4 (Emergency): 401,837 people
- Livestock losses: 456,649 animals

FAO and WFP scaling up emergency food assistance. Supplementary feeding programmes targeting 199,858 malnourished children under 5. Cash transfer programmes reaching 29,412 households.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for flood, severity: critical, location: Turkey, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Heavy monsoon rains have caused severe flooding in Istanbul, Turkey, displacing approximately 230,785 people across 11 districts. Water levels have risen to 5.1m above normal, submerging residential areas and critical infrastructure. 1710 fatalities have been confirmed so far.

HUMANITARIAN NEEDS

- Emergency shelter for 230,785 displaced persons
- Clean drinking water — municipal supply contaminated
- Medical supplies: waterborne disease prevention kits
- Food supplies for an estimated 86,151 affected individuals
- Search and rescue operations in 28 submerged neighborhoods

RESPONSE ACTIONS

National disaster management authority has declared a state of emergency. Military helicopters deployed for evacuation. International aid agencies mobilising emergency response teams. Temporary shelters established at 69 locations including schools and community centers.

RESOURCE REQUIREMENTS

- Water purification units: 73
- Emergency tents: 4,884
- Medical teams: 40
- Rescue boats: 188
- Food ration packs: 188,056

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for epidemic, severity: high, location: Syria, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

A Ebola outbreak has been declared in Syria, with 10,740 confirmed cases and 534 deaths reported across 7 provinces as of today. Case fatality rate stands at 24.1%. The outbreak originated in Aleppo and has spread to neighbouring areas.

HEALTH RESPONSE

- 43 treatment centers established
- 2,290 health workers deployed
- Contact tracing: 33,469 contacts identified and monitored
- Vaccination campaign targeting 198,160 people

CHALLENGES

Limited laboratory capacity, inadequate medical supplies, community resistance to health measures, and cross-border movement complicating containment efforts.

RESOURCE NEEDS

WHO has classified this as a Grade 1 emergency. Urgent need for medical supplies, PPE, diagnostic kits, and additional health personnel. Estimated funding gap: $94M.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for flood, severity: critical, location: Somalia, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Heavy monsoon rains have caused severe flooding in Mogadishu, Somalia, displacing approximately 394,737 people across 5 districts. Water levels have risen to 7.6m above normal, submerging residential areas and critical infrastructure. 345 fatalities have been confirmed so far.

HUMANITARIAN NEEDS

- Emergency shelter for 394,737 displaced persons
- Clean drinking water — municipal supply contaminated
- Medical supplies: waterborne disease prevention kits
- Food supplies for an estimated 33,898 affected individuals
- Search and rescue operations in 13 submerged neighborhoods

RESPONSE ACTIONS

National disaster management authority has declared a state of emergency. Military helicopters deployed for evacuation. International aid agencies mobilising emergency response teams. Temporary shelters established at 21 locations including schools and community centers.

RESOURCE REQUIREMENTS

- Water purification units: 143
- Emergency tents: 18,932
- Medical teams: 44
- Rescue boats: 122
- Food ration packs: 67,585
