records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for cyclone, severity: high, location: Kenya, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Tropical Cyclone Maria made landfall near Nairobi, Kenya as a Category 2 storm with sustained winds of 264km/h. Storm surge of 4.6m reported along the coastline. 1892 fatalities confirmed, with 442 persons still missing.

IMPACT

- 277,287 people evacuated from coastal communities
- Extensive damage to crops affecting 127,606 hectares
- Fishing fleet: 1493 vessels damaged or destroyed
- Communication networks disrupted across 8 provinces

RESPONSE PRIORITIES

1. Search and rescue in affected coastal communities
2. Emergency shelter and food distribution
3. Restoration of clean water supply
4. Medical response — injury treatment and disease prevention
5. Debris clearance for road access

RESOURCE MOBILIZATION

Red Cross/Red Crescent has deployed 21 emergency response units. WFP pre-positioned stocks being distributed. Government allocated emergency fund of $78M for immediate relief operations.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for epidemic, severity: high, location: Ethiopia, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

A measles outbreak has been declared in Ethiopia, with 20,531 confirmed cases and 1578 deaths reported across 13 provinces as of today. Case fatality rate stands at 7.7%. The outbreak originated in Addis Ababa and has spread to neighbouring areas.

HEALTH RESPONSE

- 6 treatment centers established
- 3,032 health workers deployed
- Contact tracing: 33,134 contacts identified and monitored
- Vaccination campaign targeting 729,434 people

CHALLENGES

Limited laboratory capacity, inadequate medical supplies, community resistance to health measures, and cross-border movement complicating containment efforts.

RESOURCE NEEDS

WHO has classified this as a Grade 2 emergency. Urgent need for medical supplies, PPE, diagnostic kits, and additional health personnel. Estimated funding gap: $15M.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for earthquake, severity: critical, location: Turkey, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

A magnitude 6.3 earthquake struck Istanbul, Turkey at 17:37 local time, with the epicentre located 36km underground. The quake caused widespread structural damage across the metropolitan area. 1537 deaths confirmed, 29,219 injured, and an estimated 336,267 people displaced from their homes.

DAMAGE ASSESSMENT

- 29,937 buildings collapsed or severely damaged
- Critical infrastructure: major hospital partially collapsed
- Hospitals operating at reduced capacity
- Power outages affecting 144,440 households
- Road access disrupted in 21 areas

HUMANITARIAN NEEDS

Immediate priorities include urban search and rescue, emergency medical care, temporary shelter, and restoration of water supply. Aftershocks continuing, complicating rescue efforts.

COORDINATION

UN OCHA has activated the cluster system. UNDAC team deployed. International search and rescue teams from 5 countries arriving. Government has requested international assistance.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for flood, severity: critical, location: Philippines, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Heavy monsoon rains have caused severe flooding in Manila, Philippines, displacing approximately 189,451 people across 11 districts. Water levels have risen to 7.4m above normal, submerging residential areas and critical infrastructure. 50 fatalities have been confirmed so far.

HUMANITARIAN NEEDS

- Emergency shelter for 189,451 displaced persons
- Clean drinking water — municipal supply contaminated
- Medical supplies: waterborne disease prevention kits
- Food supplies for an estimated 32,936 affected individuals
- Search and rescue operations in 26 submerged neighborhoods

RESPONSE ACTIONS

National disaster management authority has declared a state of emergency. Military helicopters deployed for evacuation. International aid agencies mobilising emergency response teams. Temporary shelters established at 11 locations including schools and community centers.

RESOURCE REQUIREMENTS

- Water purification units: 132
- Emergency tents: 2,555
- Medical teams: 30
- Rescue boats: 113
- Food ration packs: 92,436

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for epidemic, severity: high, location: Bangladesh, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

A malaria outbreak has been declared in Bangladesh, with 43,943 confirmed cases and 1520 deaths reported across 7 provinces as of today. Case fatality rate stands at 7.1%. The outbreak originated in Dhaka and has spread to neighbouring areas.

HEALTH RESPONSE

- 44 treatment centers established
- 4,057 health workers deployed
- Contact tracing: 17,944 contacts identified and monitored
- Vaccination campaign targeting 1,408,704 people

CHALLENGES

Limited laboratory capacity, inadequate medical supplies, community resistance to health measures, and cross-border movement complicating containment efforts.

RESOURCE NEEDS

WHO has classified this as a Grade 3 emergency. Urgent need for medical supplies, PPE, diagnostic kits, and additional health personnel. Estimated funding gap: $200M.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for flood, severity: critical, location: Nepal, date: 2026-03-03

Heavy monsoon rains have caused severe flooding in Kathmandu, Nepal, displacing approximately 412,392 people across 18 districts. Water levels have risen to 5.4m above normal, submerging residential areas and critical infrastructure. 112 fatalities have been confirmed so far.

- Food ration packs: 258,540

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for drought, severity: critical, location: Philippines, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

Prolonged drought conditions in Philippines have entered their 18th consecutive month, with rainfall at 24% of seasonal average. An estimated 1,474,786 people are facing acute food insecurity across 8 regions.

FOOD SECURITY

- IPC Phase 3 (Crisis): 1,004,228 people
- IPC Phase 4 (Emergency): 222,391 people
- Livestock losses: 499,213 animals
- Crop production: 42% below average

WATER SITUATION

42% of water sources dried up or critically low. Water trucking operations serving 95,909 people daily but demand far exceeds current capacity.

HUMANITARIAN RESPONSE

FAO and WFP scaling up emergency food assistance. Supplementary feeding programmes targeting 150,708 malnourished children under 5. Cash transfer programmes reaching 86,708 households.

records.instruction: Generate a situation report and response plan for earthquake, severity: critical, location: Nepal, date: 2026-03-03
records.output:
SITUATION OVERVIEW

A magnitude 6.3 earthquake struck Kathmandu, Nepal at 18:36 local time, with the epicentre located 55km underground. The quake caused widespread structural damage across the metropolitan area. 1871 deaths confirmed, 6,970 injured, and an estimated 325,591 people displaced from their homes.

DAMAGE ASSESSMENT

- 25,510 buildings collapsed or severely damaged
- Critical infrastructure: bridges damaged
- Hospitals operating at reduced capacity
- Power outages affecting 491,526 households
- Road access disrupted in 20 areas

HUMANITARIAN NEEDS

Immediate priorities include urban search and rescue, emergency medical care, temporary shelter, and restoration of water supply. Aftershocks continuing, complicating rescue efforts.

COORDINATION

UN OCHA has activated the cluster system. UNDAC team deployed. International search and rescue teams from 12 countries arriving. Government has requested international assistance.
